title: 〜からして (〜kara shite)
formation: Noun + からして
shortExplanation: Expresses that something is exceptional, unusual, or negative based on a particular aspect or point.
longExplanation: The 〜からして grammar point is used when you want to make a conclusion or emphasize that something is exceptional (often negative or surprising) from a certain aspect or starting point. The part before からして serves as the base or clue for the judgment.

sentences.jp: 彼の顔つきからして、何か悪いことがあったのだろう。
sentences.en: From his facial expression, something bad must have happened.

sentences.jp: この料理の見た目からして、おいしくなさそうだ。
sentences.en: Judging from the appearance of this dish, it doesn't look delicious.

sentences.jp: 彼女の試験成績からして、合格するのは難しいでしょう。
sentences.en: Considering her test scores, it will be difficult for her to pass.

sentences.jp: その映画のタイトルからして、面白くないと思う。
sentences.en: Judging from the title of the movie, I think it's not interesting.

id: N2-〜の上では (〜no ue de wa)
title: 〜の上では (〜no ue de wa)
formation: Noun or Phrase + の上では
shortExplanation: Indicates 'on the basis of', 'in terms of', or 'from the standpoint of'.
longExplanation: The 〜の上では grammar point is used to show that you are expressing a view or making a judgment based on a specific standpoint or perspective. It can be translated as 'on the basis of', 'in terms of', or 'from the standpoint of'. The formation is straightforward, which involves attaching 〜の上では to a noun or a phrase.

sentences.jp: 効率の上では、この方法が最適です。
sentences.en: In terms of efficiency, this method is the best.

sentences.jp: 法律の上では、彼の行為は違法ではありません。
sentences.en: From a legal standpoint, his actions are not illegal.

sentences.jp: 味の上では、このレストランは評価が高いです。
sentences.en: In terms of taste, this restaurant has a high rating.

sentences.jp: 成績の上では、彼はクラスで一番優秀です。
sentences.en: On the basis of grades, he is the most outstanding student in the class.

title: ～あげく (~ageku)
formation: Verb-た + あげく / Noun + の + あげく
shortExplanation: Expresses the outcome of a series of actions or events; 'in the end', 'finally', 'after'.
longExplanation: The ～あげく grammar point is used to express the outcome or result after a series of actions or events. It can usually be translated into English as 'in the end', 'finally', or 'after'. The typical formation is adding あげく to the past form of a verb (Verb-た), or adding のあげく to a noun. This indicates that, after a long process or a number of attempts, a certain final outcome is reached—often with a sense that it took considerable effort or time.

sentences.jp: 長い時間待ったあげく、電車が来なかった。
sentences.en: After waiting a long time, in the end, the train didn't come.

sentences.jp: いろいろな方法を試したあげく、成功した。
sentences.en: After trying various methods, I finally succeeded.

sentences.jp: 彼は失敗のあげく、会社を辞める決心をした。
sentences.en: In the end, after his failures, he decided to quit the company.

sentences.jp: 彼女は長い間悩んだあげく、その仕事を断った。
sentences.en: After worrying for a long time, she finally turned down the job.

title: ～あまり (〜amari)
formation: Verb-ます stem + あまり, い-Adjective + あまり, な-Adjective + のあまり, Noun + のあまり
shortExplanation: Expresses the result of doing something excessively or beyond a certain point.
longExplanation: The ～あまり grammar point is used to express a negative or undesirable outcome caused by doing something excessively or beyond a certain point. It often follows verbs, adjectives, and nouns, and is commonly translated as 'so much', 'too much', or 'to the extent that'.

sentences.jp: 昨日は疲れすぎたあまり、朝まで眠り続けました。
sentences.en: Yesterday, I was so tired that I slept until morning.

sentences.jp: 彼女はうれしいあまり、泣き出してしまった。
sentences.en: She was so happy that she ended up crying.

sentences.jp: 彼は安全を確認するあまり、何回もドアをチェックしている。
sentences.en: He is so concerned about safety that he checks the door many times.

sentences.jp: 寒さのあまり、彼は震えが止まらなかった。
sentences.en: It was so cold that he couldn't stop shivering.

title: ～うちに (〜uchi ni)
formation: Verb-casual + うちに, い-Adjective + うちに, な-Adjective + なうちに, Noun + のうちに
shortExplanation: Expresses doing something while a certain condition still holds; 'while', 'before', 'as long as'.
longExplanation: The ～うちに grammar point is used to indicate that an action should be done while a certain condition still exists or before a situation changes. It can be translated as 'while', 'before', or 'as long as' in English. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

sentences.jp: 元気なうちに、旅行を楽しんでください。
sentences.en: Enjoy traveling while you are healthy.

sentences.jp: 若いうちに、いろいろなことを学ぶべきだ。
sentences.en: One should learn various things while they are young.

sentences.jp: 子供が寝ているうちに、夕食の準備をしましょう。
sentences.en: Let's prepare dinner while the kids are sleeping.

sentences.jp: 雨が降らないうちに、家に帰りましょう。
sentences.en: Let's go home before it starts raining.

id: N2-～か～ないかのうちに (〜ka〜naika no uchi ni)
title: ～か～ないかのうちに (〜ka〜naika no uchi ni)
formation: Verb-casual + か + Verb-negative form + かのうちに
shortExplanation: Expresses an action that occurs immediately after another action; 'as soon as', 'no sooner than', 'the moment'.
longExplanation: The ～か～ないかのうちに grammar point is used to express that an action occurs immediately after another action. In English, it can be translated as 'as soon as', 'no sooner than', or 'the moment'. This grammar point is used with verbs, showing the verb in both affirmative and negative forms with か and ないか, followed by のうちに.

sentences.jp: 駅に着くか着かないかのうちに、雨が降り出した。
sentences.en: No sooner had I arrived at the station than it started raining.

sentences.jp: ケータイを買うか買わないかのうちに、新しいモデルが発売された。
sentences.en: As soon as I decided to buy the cell phone, a new model was released.

sentences.jp: 彼が家を出るか出ないかのうちに、電話が鳴った。
sentences.en: The moment he left the house, the phone rang.

sentences.jp: パーティーが始まるか始まらないかのうちに、彼女が泣き出した。
sentences.en: As soon as the party began, she started crying.

title: ～か～まいか (〜ka 〜maika)
formation: Verb-volitional + か + Verb-volitional + まいか, い-Adjective + であるか + い-Adjective + であるまいか, な-Adjective + であるか + な-Adjective + であるまいか, Noun + であるか + Noun + であるまいか
shortExplanation: Used to express a dilemma or indecision between two options or actions.
longExplanation: The ～か～まいか grammar point expresses uncertainty or indecision, often with a more formal or literary feel. Typically, it is seen with verbs in the volitional form plus まい (the negative volitional). In more academic or stiff writing, い／な-adjectives and nouns plus である can also appear before まいか.

sentences.jp: このプロジェクトを引き受けようか引き受けまいか、まだ決めていない。
sentences.en: I haven't decided whether to take on this project or not.

sentences.jp: 彼が成功しようか成功するまいか、今は予測できない。
sentences.en: Whether he will succeed or not is unpredictable at the moment.

sentences.jp: この問題は簡単であるか難しいであるまいか、意見が分かれている。
sentences.en: Opinions are divided on whether this problem is simple or difficult.

sentences.jp: 彼はリーダーであるかフォロワーであるまいか、自分で選ぶべきだ。
sentences.en: He should choose for himself whether he is a leader or a follower.

title: ～がい (〜gai)
formation: Verb-stem + がい
shortExplanation: Expresses 'worth doing' for verbs.
longExplanation: The ～がい grammar point is used to express that an action is worth doing or to emphasize the value and importance of doing something. It is attached to verbs in their stem form and can be translated as 'worth doing' in English.

sentences.jp: この本は読みがいがあります。
sentences.en: This book is worth reading.

sentences.jp: 早起きしがいがあると思いますよ。
sentences.en: I think it's worth waking up early.

sentences.jp: 長い時間をかけがいのある仕事だ。
sentences.en: It's a job worth spending a long time on.

sentences.jp: 彼とは話しがいがあるでしょう。
sentences.en: It's worth talking to him.

title: ～かいがあって (〜kaiga atte)
formation: Verb-past + かいがあって / Noun + の + かいがあって
shortExplanation: Used to express that an effort is worth it or has value; 'to be worth the effort' or 'to pay off'.
longExplanation: The ～かいがあって grammar point is used to convey the idea that an action or effort is worth it, or that it has value. It is often translated as 'to be worth the effort' or 'to pay off' in English. It is typically associated with actions or efforts that lead to a successful or desirable result.

sentences.jp: 毎日勉強したかいがあって、テストに合格しました。
sentences.en: Studying every day paid off—I passed the test.

sentences.jp: 練習のかいがあって、試合に勝つことができた。
sentences.en: The practice paid off, and we were able to win the match.

sentences.jp: 雨の中を走ってきたかいがあって、電車に間に合った。
sentences.en: Running in the rain was worth it, as I made it to the train on time.

sentences.jp: 早起きして掃除をしたかいがあって、部屋がきれいになった。
sentences.en: Waking up early to clean paid off; the room is now spotless.

title: ～かいもなく (〜kai mo naku)
formation: Verb-た + かいもなく / Noun + の + かいもなく
shortExplanation: Expresses the meaning of 'without worth', 'in vain', 'fruitless'.
longExplanation: The ～かいもなく grammar point is used to express that something has been done without worth, in vain, or fruitlessly. It often appears as 'Verb-た + かいもなく' or 'Noun + の + かいもなく' to indicate that an effort did not produce the hoped-for result.

sentences.jp: 長い間苦労したかいもなく、失敗してしまった。
sentences.en: I struggled for a long time, but in the end, it was all in vain and I failed.

sentences.jp: このプロジェクトにたくさん時間をかけたかいもなく、うまくいかなかった。
sentences.en: I spent a lot of time on this project, but it ended fruitlessly.

sentences.jp: 助けを求めたかいもなく、誰も来なかった。
sentences.en: He asked for help, but it was in vain as no one came.

sentences.jp: 色々な方法でダイエットを試みたかいもなく、全然痩せなかった。
sentences.en: I tried various diet methods, but it was all for nothing—I didn't lose any weight.

title: ～かける (〜kakeru)
formation: Verb-stem + かける
shortExplanation: Indicates a process or action is 'in progress' or 'halfway'.
longExplanation: The ～かける grammar point is used to describe a process or action that is in progress, halfway, or not yet completed. It can be attached to verbs to imply that the action is still ongoing or something is in a certain state of partial completion.

sentences.jp: 彼女は食べかけのリンゴを置いて出かけた。
sentences.en: She left a partially eaten apple and went out.

sentences.jp: 子供たちはやりかけの宿題を机の上に残した。
sentences.en: The children left their half-finished homework on the desk.

sentences.jp: 読みかけの本を夜になると探す。
sentences.en: I look for the book I started reading once night comes.

sentences.jp: オフィスには、作りかけの書類がたくさんある。
sentences.en: There are a lot of documents in the process of being created in the office.

title: ～がち (〜gachi)
formation: Verb-ます stem + がち / い-Adjective stem + がち / な-Adjective stem + がち
shortExplanation: Expresses that something tends to occur frequently or habitually.
longExplanation: The ～がち grammar point is used to express that something has a tendency to occur frequently or habitually. It can be attached to verbs and adjectives to indicate that the action or state tends to occur often.

sentences.jp: 彼は風邪を引きがちです。
sentences.en: He tends to catch colds easily.

sentences.jp: この地域は雨が降りがちです。
sentences.en: This area tends to get a lot of rain.

sentences.jp: 彼女は忘れ物をしがちです。
sentences.en: She tends to forget things.

sentences.jp: 私は遅刻しがちなので、もっと早く起きないといけません。
sentences.en: I tend to be late, so I need to wake up earlier.

title: ～かねない (〜kane nai)
formation: Verb-stem + かねない
shortExplanation: Express the likelihood of a negative outcome or consequence.
longExplanation: The ～かねない grammar point is used to express the possibility or likelihood of a negative outcome or consequence, often used when there is concern about the result. It is typically used with verbs in their stem form and can be understood as 'might (have a bad result)'.

sentences.jp: 彼はあまりにも無責任で、約束を破りかねない。
sentences.en: He is so irresponsible that he might break his promise.

sentences.jp: 注意しないと、事故を起こしかねない。
sentences.en: If you don't pay attention, you might cause an accident.

sentences.jp: このアプリはバグが多いので、クラッシュしかねない。
sentences.en: Since this app has many bugs, it might crash.

sentences.jp: 彼女は緊張しているので、失敗しかねない。
sentences.en: She's so nervous that she might fail.

title: ～かねる (〜kaneru)
formation: Verb-ます stem + かねる
shortExplanation: Express inability or impossibility of doing an action (often as a polite refusal).
longExplanation: The ～かねる grammar point is used to express that someone cannot do an action or that something is impossible to do. It typically has a nuance of reluctance or polite refusal and is formed by adding かねる to the ます-stem of the verb.

sentences.jp: その問題は難しいので、私には解決しかねます。
sentences.en: Since that problem is difficult, I cannot solve it.

sentences.jp: 彼の言っていることが真実かどうか判断しかねます。
sentences.en: I cannot judge whether what he is saying is true or not.

sentences.jp: 彼女が来るかどうか今すぐには分かりかねます。
sentences.en: I cannot immediately know whether she will come or not.

sentences.jp: 詳しい内容については説明しかねます。
sentences.en: I cannot explain the details.

id: N2-～かのようだ (〜ka no you da)
title: ～かのようだ (〜ka no you da)
formation: Verb-casual + かのようだ, い-Adjective + かのようだ, な-Adjective + であるかのようだ, Noun + であるかのようだ
shortExplanation: Express a similarity or comparison; 'as if', 'like', 'as though'.
longExplanation: The ～かのようだ grammar point is used to express similarity or comparison between two things or situations. It can be translated as 'as if', 'like', or 'as though' in English. The standard patterns differ depending on whether it’s used with verbs, い-adjectives, な-adjectives, or nouns. For な-adjectives and nouns, we usually insert である before かのようだ.

sentences.jp: 彼は犬が怖いかのようだ。
sentences.en: He seems as if he is scared of dogs.

sentences.jp: この部屋は寒いかのようだ。
sentences.en: This room feels as if it's cold.

sentences.jp: 彼女は有名人であるかのようだ。
sentences.en: She acts as though she is a celebrity.

sentences.jp: 彼は子供であるかのようだ。
sentences.en: He acts as if he's a child.

title: ～から～にかけて (〜kara 〜ni kakete)
formation: Noun 1 + から + Noun 2 + にかけて
shortExplanation: Expresses a range of time, space, or quantities; 'from...to', 'between...and', 'over'.
longExplanation: The ～から～にかけて grammar pattern is used to indicate a range or scope—often in time or space. It can be translated as 'from...to', 'between...and', or 'over' in English.

sentences.jp: 午前９時から午後５時にかけて、仕事をしています。
sentences.en: I work from 9 in the morning to 5 in the evening.

sentences.jp: 東京から大阪にかけて、新幹線が走っています。
sentences.en: The bullet train runs from Tokyo to Osaka.

sentences.jp: 春から夏にかけて、花がたくさん咲いています。
sentences.en: Many flowers bloom between spring and summer.

sentences.jp: ２０歳から３０歳にかけて、多くの人が大学を卒業し、仕事に就くことが一般的です。
sentences.en: It is common for many people to graduate from university and start working between the ages of 20 and 30.

title: ～からこそ (〜kara koso)
formation: Verb-casual + からこそ, い-Adjective + からこそ, な-Adjective + だからこそ, Noun + だからこそ
shortExplanation: Express the emphasis on the reason; 'precisely because', 'it is precisely because'.
longExplanation: The ～からこそ grammar point is used to emphasize the reason or cause of a situation, often implying that the effect is especially notable or even surprising. It can be translated as 'precisely because' or 'it is precisely because.'

sentences.jp: 失敗したからこそ、成功の意味がわかる。
sentences.en: It is precisely because I failed that I understand the meaning of success.

sentences.jp: 苦しかったからこそ、今日の私がある。
sentences.en: It is precisely because I went through hardships that I am who I am today.

sentences.jp: 彼は親切だからこそ、人気がある。
sentences.en: He is popular precisely because he is kind.

sentences.jp: 昔の経験があるからこそ、その問題がよく理解できる。
sentences.en: I can understand that problem well precisely because I have past experience.

title: ～からすると (〜kara suru to)
formation: Noun + からすると
shortExplanation: Expresses a judgment or evaluation based on a certain standard; 'from this perspective', 'considering', 'judging from'.
longExplanation: The ～からすると grammar point is used to make a judgment or evaluation based on some criterion, perspective, or standard. It can be translated as 'from this perspective', 'considering', or 'judging from' in English.

sentences.jp: 経験からすると、この仕事は難しいでしょう。
sentences.en: Considering their experience, this job might be difficult.

sentences.jp: 評判からすると、あのレストランはとても美味しいです。
sentences.en: Judging from the reputation, that restaurant is very delicious.

sentences.jp: 価格からすると、この商品は高品質のものと思われます。
sentences.en: Considering the price, this product seems to be of high quality.

sentences.jp: このエリアですが、交通の便からすると、便利です。
sentences.en: As for this area, considering the transportation convenience, it's convenient.

id: N2-～からといって (〜kara to itte)
title: ～からといって (〜kara to itte)
formation: Verb-casual + からといって, い-Adjective + からといって, な-Adjective + だからといって, Noun + だからといって
shortExplanation: Expresses 'just because...doesn't mean' or 'even though...not necessarily'.
longExplanation: The ～からといって grammar point is used to convey that even though something is true, it does not necessarily mean a certain result will occur. It can be translated as 'just because...doesn't mean' or 'even though...not necessarily.'

sentences.jp: 雨が降ったからといって、試合が中止になるわけではありません。
sentences.en: Just because it rained doesn't mean the game will be canceled.

sentences.jp: 彼が有名だからといって、彼の意見がいつも正しいわけではない。
sentences.en: Just because he is famous doesn't mean his opinions are always correct.

sentences.jp: このスープが辛いからといって、全ての人が辛いと感じるわけではない。
sentences.en: Just because this soup is spicy doesn't mean everyone will find it spicy.

sentences.jp: 彼女が泣いているからといって、悲しいわけではないかもしれない。
sentences.en: Just because she is crying doesn't necessarily mean she's sad.

title: ～からには (〜kara niwa)
formation: Noun + だ + からには, な-Adjective + だ + からには, い-Adjective + からには, Verb-casual + からには
shortExplanation: Express a sense of responsibility or determination; 'since', 'now that', 'if'.
longExplanation: The ～からには grammar point is used to show a sense of responsibility or strong resolve once a certain condition is met. It can be translated as 'since', 'now that', or 'if'.

sentences.jp: 試験に合格したからには、一所懸命働かなければならない。
sentences.en: Since I passed the exam, I have to work hard.

sentences.jp: 約束したからには、守らなければなりません。
sentences.en: Since I made a promise, I must keep it.

sentences.jp: この映画が面白いからには、友達にも見せたい。
sentences.en: Since this movie is interesting, I want to show it to my friends too.

sentences.jp: 彼女が助けを求めたからには、手伝わなければならない。
sentences.en: Since she asked for help, I must help her.

title: ～から見ると (〜kara miru to)
formation: Noun + から見ると
shortExplanation: Express a perspective or point of view; 'from the standpoint of', 'in terms of', 'from the perspective of'.
longExplanation: The ～から見ると grammar point is used to express a particular perspective or point of view. In English, it translates to 'from the standpoint of', 'from the perspective of', or 'in terms of'. It is usually used with a noun that indicates the perspective.

sentences.jp: 費用から見ると、このプランはお得です。
sentences.en: In terms of cost, this plan is a good deal.

sentences.jp: 彼女の意見から見ると、社長の決定は理解できる。
sentences.en: From her perspective, the president's decision is understandable.

sentences.jp: 実力から見ると、彼はチームで最も優れている。
sentences.en: In terms of ability, he is the best in the team.

sentences.jp: 健康面から見ると、この食品は良くない。
sentences.en: From the standpoint of health, this food is not good.

title: ～から言うと (〜kara iuto)
formation: Noun + から言うと (common), [Verb-casual / Adjective + から言って] (less common)
shortExplanation: Express a judgment or opinion based on a specific criterion; 'speaking from', 'in terms of', 'from the viewpoint of'.
longExplanation: The ～から言うと grammar point is used to express a judgment or opinion based on a specific criterion or aspect. It can be translated as 'speaking from', 'in terms of', or 'from the viewpoint of' in English. It is most commonly used with nouns, although you may occasionally see other forms (like adjectives).

sentences.jp: 値段から言うと、この商品が最もお得です。
sentences.en: Speaking from the viewpoint of price, this product is the best deal.

sentences.jp: 味から言って、このレストランはおいしいと思います。
sentences.en: In terms of taste, I think this restaurant is delicious.

sentences.jp: 便利さから言って、このアパートは最適な場所にあります。
sentences.en: Speaking from the perspective of convenience, this apartment is in the best location.

sentences.jp: 健康から言うと、毎日運動することが大切です。
sentences.en: From the viewpoint of health, it is important to exercise every day.

title: ～くせに (〜kuse ni)
formation: Verb (casual) + くせに / い-Adjective + くせに / な-Adjective + な(or の) + くせに / Noun + の + くせに
shortExplanation: Used to criticize someone for doing something contrary to what one would expect from their nature or situation.
longExplanation: The grammar point ～くせに is used to express criticism or disapproval toward someone who does something contradictory to their expected nature, role, or prior statement. It's used in fairly casual or spoken contexts and can sound quite critical or accusing. Note that there's a colloquial variant ～くせして, but ～くせに is the standard form taught in most references.

sentences.jp: 彼はいつも遅刻してくるくせに、自分が待たされるのが嫌いだ。
sentences.en: He’s always late, yet he hates being kept waiting.

sentences.jp: 悪いなと言ってるくせに、また同じことを繰り返している。
sentences.en: He says he's sorry, yet he keeps doing the same thing.

sentences.jp: 彼は背が高いくせに、バスケが下手だ。
sentences.en: Despite being tall, he's bad at basketball.

sentences.jp: この店は有名なのくせに、サービスが悪い。
sentences.en: Despite being famous, this store has poor service.

title: ～ことから (〜koto kara)
formation: Verb-casual + ことから / い-Adjective + ことから / な-Adjective + なことから / Noun + のことから
shortExplanation: Indicates the reason or basis for something; 'because', 'since', 'due to'.
longExplanation: The ～ことから grammar point is used to indicate the reason or basis for a conclusion, fact, or premise. It can be translated as 'because', 'since', or 'due to' in English. The same pattern is used whether it’s with verbs, adjectives, or nouns (with the appropriate こと or の).

sentences.jp: 彼は毎日運動することから、体が丈夫です。
sentences.en: Because he exercises every day, he is physically strong.

sentences.jp: この店のサービスがいいことから、いつも混んでいます。
sentences.en: This store is always crowded because of its good service.

sentences.jp: 彼女は親切なことから、みんなに好かれています。
sentences.en: She’s well-liked by everyone due to her kindness.

sentences.jp: その会社の評判が良いことから、就職先として人気があります。
sentences.en: Because that company’s reputation is good, it’s popular as a place to work.

title: ～ことに (〜koto ni)
formation: Verb-casual + ことに / い-Adjective + ことに / な-Adjective + なことに / Noun + のことに
shortExplanation: Used to express feelings or emotions about a situation, action, or outcome.
longExplanation: The ～ことに grammar point is used to highlight the speaker’s feelings or emotional reaction toward a situation, action, or outcome. It often appears in constructions like 驚いたことに, 残念なことに, 不思議なことに, etc.

sentences.jp: 彼が急に帰国することに驚いた。
sentences.en: I was surprised that he suddenly returned to his home country.

sentences.jp: 彼女が優勝したことに感動した。
sentences.en: I was moved by the fact that she won the championship.

sentences.jp: 彼がいつも遅刻することに腹が立つ。
sentences.en: I get angry that he is always late.

sentences.jp: 彼女がこんなに美しいことに気がつかなかった。
sentences.en: I hadn’t realized how beautiful she was.

id: N2-～ことになっている (〜koto ni natte iru)
title: ～ことになっている (〜koto ni natte iru)
formation: Verb-plain form + ことになっている / い-Adjective + ことになっている / な-Adjective + だ + ことになっている / Noun + だ + ことになっている
shortExplanation: Expresses that something is supposed to be or has been decided (rule, plan, custom).
longExplanation: The ～ことになっている grammar point is used when something has been decided, planned, or there is a rule/custom that everyone follows. It can be translated as 'it is supposed to be,' 'it is decided,' or 'it is customary that.'

sentences.jp: 日本では、左側を運転することになっています。
sentences.en: In Japan, it is decided that people drive on the left side.

sentences.jp: 会社の規則では、制服を着ることになっている。
sentences.en: According to the company rules, we are supposed to wear uniforms.

sentences.jp: 彼女は明日結婚することになっています。
sentences.en: She is supposed to get married tomorrow.

sentences.jp: この商品は高品質だということになっています。
sentences.en: It is said (or generally accepted) that this product is of high quality.

title: ～さえ～ば (〜sae ~ba)
formation: Noun + さえ + Verb-conditional / Verb-ますstem + さえすれば / etc. (varies by part of speech)
shortExplanation: Expresses that, as long as a certain condition is met, something else will follow; 'if only', 'provided that'.
longExplanation: The ～さえ～ば grammar point is used to indicate that if a certain single condition is fulfilled, everything else will be fine or a certain result will occur. It often translates as 'as long as...' or 'if only...'.

sentences.jp: お金さえあれば、幸せです。
sentences.en: As long as I have money, I’m happy.

sentences.jp: 試験に合格しさえすれば、卒業できます。
sentences.en: If only I pass the exam, I can graduate.

sentences.jp: 野菜さえたくさん食べれば、母は喜びます。
sentences.en: As long as I eat plenty of vegetables, my mother will be happy.

sentences.jp: 交通費さえ支払ってもらえれば、どこへでも行きます。
sentences.en: As long as my transportation costs are covered, I’ll go anywhere.

id: N2-～ざるを得ない (〜zaru wo enai)
title: ～ざるを得ない (〜zaru wo enai)
formation: Verb-ない form (drop ない) + ざるを得ない
shortExplanation: Have no choice but to; cannot help but.
longExplanation: The ～ざるを得ない grammar point is used to indicate that one has no choice but to do something or cannot avoid doing it. It is formed from the negative form (ない) but replaces ない with ざる, followed by を得ない. (Note: する → せざるを得ない.)

sentences.jp: この仕事は締め切りが近いので、残業せざるを得ない。
sentences.en: Since the deadline is near, I have no choice but to work overtime.

sentences.jp: 電車が遅れたため、タクシーに乗るざるを得なかった。
sentences.en: Because the train was delayed, I had no choice but to take a taxi.

sentences.jp: 彼女の説明が納得できなかったが、とりあえず信じざるを得ない。
sentences.en: I couldn’t really accept her explanation, but for now I have no choice but to believe her.

sentences.jp: この状況では、新しい計画を立て直さざるを得ない。
sentences.en: In this situation, we have no choice but to revise the new plan.

id: N2-～ずにはいられない (〜zu ni wa irarenai)
title: ～ずにはいられない (〜zu ni wa irarenai)
formation: Verb-ない form (drop ない) + ずにはいられない
shortExplanation: Cannot help but; cannot resist doing; an irresistible urge.
longExplanation: The ～ずにはいられない grammar point expresses that one cannot resist or help doing something. In other words, there is a strong urge that one cannot suppress. It's formed from the negative stem of a verb plus ずにはいられない. (Note: する → せずにはいられない.)

sentences.jp: この曲を聞くと、歌わずにはいられない。
sentences.en: When I hear this song, I can’t help but sing.

sentences.jp: 彼女が泣いているのを見ると、慰めずにはいられない。
sentences.en: When I see her crying, I can’t resist comforting her.

sentences.jp: この小説は面白いので、読まずにはいられない。
sentences.en: This novel is so interesting that I can’t help but read it.

sentences.jp: 彼はおいしい料理を前にすると、食べずにはいられない。
sentences.en: When he’s faced with delicious food, he can’t help but eat it.

title: ～そうにない (〜sou ni nai)
formation: Verb-stem + そうにない / い-Adjective (drop い) + そうにない / な-Adjective + そうにない
shortExplanation: Express the unlikelihood of something happening; 'not likely', 'doesn't seem'.
longExplanation: The ～そうにない grammar point is used to indicate that something does not appear likely to happen. Often translated as 'doesn't seem like' or 'not likely to...'. Attach そうにない to the verb stem or adjective stem.

sentences.jp: この雨は止みそうにない。
sentences.en: It doesn't seem like this rain will stop.

sentences.jp: 彼が試験に合格しそうにない。
sentences.en: It doesn't look like he'll pass the exam.

sentences.jp: この料理がおいしそうにない。
sentences.en: This dish doesn't look like it will be tasty.

sentences.jp: この部屋はきれいそうにない。
sentences.en: This room doesn't seem likely to be clean.

id: N2-～たかと思ったら (〜ta ka to omottara)
title: ～たかと思ったら (〜ta ka to omottara)
formation: Verb-た form + かと思ったら
shortExplanation: Express a sequence of events happening in quick succession; 'as soon as', 'no sooner than'.
longExplanation: The ～たかと思ったら grammar point indicates that one action occurs almost immediately after another, often conveying surprise at how quickly it happens. It is frequently translated as 'as soon as...' or 'no sooner than...'.

sentences.jp: 電話をかけたかと思ったら、すぐに彼女が出た。
sentences.en: No sooner had I called than she picked up.

sentences.jp: 離陸したかと思ったら、飛行機が揺れ始めた。
sentences.en: As soon as we took off, the plane started shaking.

sentences.jp: 買い物に行ったかと思ったら、雨が降ってきた。
sentences.en: No sooner had I gone shopping than it began to rain.

sentences.jp: 仕事を終わったかと思ったら、上司が追加の仕事を持ってきた。
sentences.en: As soon as I finished work, my boss brought more tasks.

title: ～たきり (〜takiri)
formation: Verb-past + きり
shortExplanation: Express 'since then' or 'after'; used to indicate that an action occurred once and has not been repeated or that a state has continued since a certain event or time.
longExplanation: The ～たきり grammar point is used to express that an action occurred once and has not been repeated, or that a situation or state has continued since a certain event or time. It can be translated as 'since then', 'after', or 'one time only' in English.

sentences.jp: 彼は会社を辞めたきり、連絡がない。
sentences.en: Ever since he quit the company, there's been no contact.

sentences.jp: 昨日の朝、メールを送ったきり、返事がない。
sentences.en: I sent an email yesterday morning, and there's been no reply since then.

sentences.jp: 子供のころ海に行ったきりで、もう20年になる。
sentences.en: It's been 20 years since I last went to the sea as a child.

sentences.jp: 彼女と別れたきり、彼女のことを考えなくなった。
sentences.en: Ever since I broke up with her, I stopped thinking about her.

title: ～だけあって (〜dake atte)
formation: Verb-casual + だけあって, い-Adjective + だけあって, な-Adjective + なだけあって, Noun + だけあって
shortExplanation: Expresses that something is as expected or deserves its reputation.
longExplanation: The ～だけあって grammar point is used to indicate that something is as expected or deserves its reputation, based on certain facts or qualities. It can be translated as 'no wonder', 'as expected', or 'it is natural that' in English.

sentences.jp: 彼は日本語が上手だけあって、日本人のように話すことができる。
sentences.en: As expected from his good Japanese skills, he can speak like a native.

sentences.jp: このレストランは有名だけあって、料理がとても美味しい。
sentences.en: No wonder this restaurant is famous; the food is very delicious.

sentences.jp: 彼女は経験豊富なだけあって、その問題を簡単に解決できた。
sentences.en: It's natural that she's experienced; she solved the problem easily.

sentences.jp: 彼は優秀な学生だけあって、いつもテストの点数が高い。
sentences.en: As expected of an excellent student, he always scores high on tests.

title: ～だけましだ (〜dake mashi da)
formation: Verb-casual + だけましだ, い-Adjective + だけましだ, な-Adjective + だけましだ, Noun + だけましだ
shortExplanation: Expresses that although the situation isn’t ideal, it could be worse; 'it’s not great, but at least...'.
longExplanation: The ～だけましだ grammar point is used to compare two undesirable or less-than-ideal situations, saying that one is not good, but still better than the worse alternative. It often translates as 'at least (it’s only...)' or 'it could be worse.'

sentences.jp: 失敗するだけましだ。もっと大変なことにならなくてよかった。
sentences.en: At least we only failed—it could’ve been much worse.

sentences.jp: 遅れるだけましだ。来られないよりはいい。
sentences.en: At least I’m only late. It’s better than not being able to come at all.

sentences.jp: 小さな部屋だけましだ。全く部屋がないよりはいい。
sentences.en: At least we have a small room; it’s better than having no room at all.

sentences.jp: 彼が結婚しないだけましだ。大変なことになるところだったよ。
sentences.en: At least he’s not getting married—things could have gotten complicated.

title: ～たところ (〜ta tokoro)
formation: Verb-past + ところ
shortExplanation: Used to describe an action that has just been completed, often followed by a result or observation.
longExplanation: The ～たところ grammar point is used when an action has just been completed and the speaker wants to describe an observation or result that follows. It can be translated as 'just finished doing something' or 'as soon as (I did something)...'.

sentences.jp: 掃除したところ、部屋がずっとキレイになりました。
sentences.en: I just finished cleaning, and the room is so much cleaner now.

sentences.jp: 彼に聞いたところ、その質問の答えは分からないみたいです。
sentences.en: I just asked him, and it seems he doesn't know the answer.

sentences.jp: 宿題を出したところ、先生が間違いを見つけました。
sentences.en: As soon as I turned in my homework, the teacher found a mistake.

sentences.jp: 昼ごはんを食べたところ、気分が良くなりました。
sentences.en: Right after eating lunch, I started feeling better.

title: ～たとたん (〜ta totan)
formation: Verb-past + たとたん
shortExplanation: Express the moment something happens; 'as soon as', 'just when'.
longExplanation: The ～たとたん grammar point is used to express that an action or event occurred at the same moment another action or event happened. It can be translated as 'as soon as' or 'just when.' It requires the past tense of a verb.

sentences.jp: ドアを開けたとたん、雨が降り始めた。
sentences.en: As soon as I opened the door, it started raining.

sentences.jp: 電話をかけたとたん、バッテリーが切れた。
sentences.en: Just when I made the phone call, the battery died.

sentences.jp: 彼女に会ったとたん、思い出の歌が流れた。
sentences.en: No sooner had I met her than a nostalgic song started playing.

sentences.jp: テストが終わったとたん、リラックスできた。
sentences.en: The moment the test ended, I was able to relax.

title: ～だらけ (〜darake)
formation: Noun + だらけ
shortExplanation: Indicates that something is full of or covered with a certain (usually negative) characteristic.
longExplanation: The ～だらけ grammar point is used to express that something is full of, covered with, or filled with a certain characteristic. It frequently has a negative or undesirable nuance.

sentences.jp: 彼の部屋はゴミだらけだ。
sentences.en: His room is full of trash.

sentences.jp: このビーチは岩だらけで泳ぎにくい。
sentences.en: This beach is full of rocks and is difficult to swim at.

sentences.jp: 彼の顔はしわだらけだ。
sentences.en: His face is covered with wrinkles.

sentences.jp: このリンゴは虫食いだらけだ。
sentences.en: This apple is riddled with wormholes.

title: ～っこない (〜kkonai)
formation: Verb-negative stem + っこない
shortExplanation: Expresses the impossibility or very low probability of something happening; 'no way', 'impossible'.
longExplanation: The ～っこない grammar point is used to express that something is impossible or extremely unlikely. It’s formed by attaching ～っこない to the negative stem of a verb. It can be translated as 'no way', 'no chance', or 'impossible.'

sentences.jp: こんなに雪が降っているんだから、学校に間に合いっこない。
sentences.en: With snow falling this heavily, there's no way we'll make it to school on time.

sentences.jp: 彼は泳げないので、プールの向こう側に泳いで行きっこない。
sentences.en: Since he can’t swim, there’s no way he’ll make it to the other side of the pool.

sentences.jp: 彼女はとても怖がりなので、一人で夜の公園に行きっこない。
sentences.en: She’s so timid, there’s no chance she’d go to the park alone at night.

sentences.jp: この短い時間で、その本を全部読むことはできっこない。
sentences.en: It's impossible to read the entire book in such a short time.

title: ～つつ (〜tsutsu)
formation: Verb-stem + つつ
shortExplanation: Shows two actions happening simultaneously or a contrast; 'while', 'although'.
longExplanation: The ～つつ grammar point is used to express two actions occurring simultaneously, or to show a contrast between an ongoing action and another factor. It can be translated as 'while' or 'although.' It is attached to a verb in its stem form.

sentences.jp: 彼はテレビを見つつ、宿題をしていました。
sentences.en: He was doing his homework while watching TV.

sentences.jp: 最近忙しいと思いつつ、毎日運動を続けています。
sentences.en: Although I feel busy these days, I still exercise every day.

sentences.jp: 彼女は泣きつつ、笑顔で別れを告げた。
sentences.en: She was crying, yet she said goodbye with a smile on her face.

sentences.jp: 雨に濡れつつ、彼は歩き続けました。
sentences.en: He continued walking even though he was getting soaked by the rain.

title: ～つつある (〜tsutsu aru)
formation: Verb-stem + つつある
shortExplanation: Expresses an ongoing process or gradual change.
longExplanation: The ～つつある grammar point is used to describe something that is in the middle of a gradual change or development. It can be translated as '(something) is in the process of ~ing.' The most common usage is with the verb stem plus つつある.

sentences.jp: 地球温暖化は進行しつつある。
sentences.en: Global warming is in progress.

sentences.jp: この町は急速に発展しつつある。
sentences.en: This town is rapidly developing.

sentences.jp: 子供たちは成長しつつある。
sentences.en: The children are growing up.

sentences.jp: 景気が回復しつつある。
sentences.en: The economy is recovering.

title: ～っぱなし (〜ppanashi)
formation: Verb-ます stem + っぱなし
shortExplanation: Express a continuous action or state; 'left as is', 'non-stop'.
longExplanation: The ～っぱなし grammar point is used to express that something is left in a certain condition for an extended time or that an action continues without stopping. It attaches to the ます-stem of verbs.

sentences.jp: 窓を開けっぱなしにして、部屋が寒くなってしまった。
sentences.en: I left the window open, and the room got cold.

sentences.jp: 彼は音楽を聴くのが好きで、いつもイヤホンをつけっぱなしです。
sentences.en: He loves listening to music and always has his earphones on.

sentences.jp: 子供はおもちゃを床に置きっぱなしで、片付けなかった。
sentences.en: The child left the toys on the floor and didn’t put them away.

sentences.jp: 昨夜は忙しかったので、テレビをつけっぱなしにして寝てしまった。
sentences.en: I was busy last night, so I left the TV on and fell asleep.

title: ～っぽい (〜ppoi)
formation: Noun + っぽい, Verb-casual + っぽい, い-Adjective + っぽい, な-Adjective + っぽい
shortExplanation: Indicates that something has the appearance, characteristics, or behavior of something else.
longExplanation: The ～っぽい grammar point is used when something or someone appears to have characteristics, traits, or a vibe that resemble something else. It can mean 'looks like,' 'seems like,' or 'has the quality of.'

sentences.jp: 彼女は大人っぽい服を着ている。
sentences.en: She’s wearing clothes that look mature.

sentences.jp: この説明は分かりにくっぽい。
sentences.en: This explanation seems hard to understand.

sentences.jp: 彼は疲れたっぽい顔をしている。
sentences.en: He has a face that looks tired.

sentences.jp: この部屋は寒っぽい感じがする。
sentences.en: This room feels kind of chilly.

title: ～ていられない (〜te irarenai)
formation: Verb-て form + いられない
shortExplanation: Expresses that one cannot afford to do something; 'can't keep on', 'unable to continue'.
longExplanation: The ～ていられない grammar point is used to express a feeling of not being able to continue doing something or remaining in a certain state. It can be translated as 'can't keep on', 'unable to continue', or 'can't afford to' in English. The formation is the same for all verb types.

sentences.jp: こんなに忙しいのに、無理に遊んでいられない。
sentences.en: I'm so busy that I can't afford to just play around.

sentences.jp: 寒すぎて、外で待っていられない。
sentences.en: It's too cold; I can't keep waiting outside.

sentences.jp: この問題は重要だから、放っておいていられない。
sentences.en: This issue is too important to ignore.

sentences.jp: 彼女の態度が我慢できず、黙っていられない。
sentences.en: I can't stand her attitude, so I can't stay silent.

title: ～てかなわない (〜te kanawanai)
formation: Adjective (〜く / 〜で) + て + かなわない / Verb phrase (reworded to an adjective/negative) + て + かなわない
shortExplanation: Expresses that something cannot be endured, tolerated, or is unbearable.
longExplanation: The ～てかなわない grammar point is typically used with adjectives (or a rephrased verb) in 〜て form to show an unbearable situation or feeling. It means you 'can’t stand' or 'can’t endure' something. It often appears with expressions like 「暑くてかなわない」「うるさくてかなわない」 and so on.

sentences.jp: こんなに暑くてかなわない。
sentences.en: I can’t stand this heat.

sentences.jp: 彼の話は失礼すぎてかなわない。
sentences.en: His remarks are so rude I can’t put up with them.

sentences.jp: 映画があまりに感動的で、忘れられなくてかなわない。
sentences.en: The movie was so moving that I can’t stand how it stays on my mind.

sentences.jp: 彼女の笑顔がまぶしくてかなわない。
sentences.en: Her smile is so dazzling that it’s overwhelming.

id: N2-～てからでないと (〜te kara denai to)
title: ～てからでないと (〜te kara denai to)
formation: Verb-て + からでないと
shortExplanation: Indicates that something cannot be done before a certain action is completed; 'not until', 'only after'.
longExplanation: The ～てからでないと grammar point is used to express that an action cannot be done before another action has been completed. It can be translated as 'not until' or 'only after' in English. It attaches to a verb in the て-form, plus からでないと.

sentences.jp: 宿題をしてからでないと、遊びに行けません。
sentences.en: You cannot go play until you’ve finished your homework.

sentences.jp: 食事をしてからでないと、デザートを食べられません。
sentences.en: You cannot eat dessert until after having a meal.

sentences.jp: チケットを買ってからでないと、映画を見られません。
sentences.en: You can’t watch the movie until after you buy a ticket.

sentences.jp: 試験が終わってからでないと、休みを取れません。
sentences.en: You can’t take time off until the exam is over.

title: ～てこそ (〜te koso)
formation: Verb-て form + こそ / い-Adjective + くてこそ / な-Adjective + でこそ / Noun + でこそ
shortExplanation: Emphasizes the condition; 'only when', 'precisely because'.
longExplanation: The ～てこそ grammar point is used to emphasize that 'only when' or 'precisely because' the stated condition is met, a certain result or meaning arises. For nouns and adjectives, similar forms appear (e.g., Noun + でこそ, い-Adjective くてこそ, な-Adjective でこそ).

sentences.jp: 勉強してこそ、テストに合格できる。
sentences.en: Only when you study can you pass the test.

sentences.jp: 素直になってこそ、相手の気持ちが分かります。
sentences.en: Only when you’re honest can you understand the other person’s feelings.

sentences.jp: 静かでこそ、心が落ち着く。
sentences.en: Only in silence can one’s mind be at peace.

sentences.jp: 友達でこそ、お互いに助け合うのは当然だ。
sentences.en: Precisely because we’re friends, it’s natural to help each other.

id: N2-～でしょうがない (〜deshou ga nai)
title: ～でしょうがない (〜deshou ga nai)
formation: い-Adjective(く) + てしょうがない / な-Adjective + で(は)しょうがない / Verb-て form + しょうがない
shortExplanation: Expresses an intense emotional or physical state; 'extremely', 'can’t help but'.
longExplanation: The pattern ～(て)しょうがない (or 〜でしょうがない) indicates a condition or feeling so intense that one is almost 'overcome' by it. It’s often used with emotions (e.g. 退屈で(しょうがない), 嬉しくてしょうがない, 寒くてしょうがない).

sentences.jp: 今日は暇で(は)しょうがない。
sentences.en: I’m extremely bored today.

sentences.jp: 試験が近いから不安でしょうがない。
sentences.en: I’m so anxious because the exam is coming up.

sentences.jp: 頭が痛くてしょうがないから、早退したい。
sentences.en: My head hurts so much I can’t stand it—I want to leave early.

sentences.jp: 新しいゲームが気になってしょうがない。
sentences.en: I can’t help but keep thinking about the new game.

title: ～でたまらない (〜de tamaranai)
formation: い-Adjective (く) + てたまらない / な-Adjective + でたまらない / Verb-て form + たまらない
shortExplanation: Express extremely strong feelings or sensations; 'can't stand', 'unbearably'.
longExplanation: The ～でたまらない grammar point is used to convey very strong emotion or sensation—so strong it’s almost unbearable. It’s common with expressions like 暑くてたまらない, うれしくてたまらない, etc.

sentences.jp: この暑さは本当に暑くてたまらない。
sentences.en: This heat is absolutely unbearable.

sentences.jp: 彼女のことが好きでたまらない。
sentences.en: I like her so much I can hardly stand it.

sentences.jp: この部屋は寒くてたまらない。
sentences.en: This room is unbearably cold.

sentences.jp: 彼の態度を見ていると、腹が立ってたまらない。
sentences.en: Seeing his attitude makes me extremely angry.

title: ～でならない (〜de naranai)
formation: な-Adjective + でならない / い-Adjective (く) + てならない / Verb-て + ならない
shortExplanation: Indicates an uncontrollable emotion or feeling; 'can’t help but', 'overwhelmingly'.
longExplanation: The ～でならない grammar pattern expresses a very strong, often involuntary feeling—e.g., '不思議でならない' (it’s so mysterious I can’t help thinking about it), '残念でならない' (I’m so disappointed), '心配でならない' (I’m extremely worried).

sentences.jp: 彼の演技が素晴らしくてならない。
sentences.en: I can’t help but be amazed at his wonderful performance.

sentences.jp: 合格発表が気になってならない。
sentences.en: I can’t stop worrying about the exam results.

sentences.jp: 友達が引っ越してしまって、寂しくてならない。
sentences.en: My friend moved away, and I feel unbearably lonely.

sentences.jp: 初めての海外旅行で、不安でならない。
sentences.en: It’s my first trip abroad, and I’m extremely anxious.

id: N2-～でばかりはいられない (〜de bakari wa irarenai)
title: ～でばかりはいられない (〜de bakari wa irarenai)
formation: Verb-て form + ばかりはいられない / Noun + ばかりはいられない (with appropriate particles)
shortExplanation: Cannot afford to only do something; cannot always rely on it.
longExplanation: The ～でばかりはいられない grammar point is used when one realizes they cannot keep doing only that one thing or cannot always rely on a certain situation. It implies the need to take other actions or responsibilities.

sentences.jp: いつも助けてもらってばかりはいられない。
sentences.en: I can’t just keep letting people help me all the time.

sentences.jp: 好きな仕事ばかりしてはいられない。
sentences.en: I can’t afford to only do the work I love.

sentences.jp: 親に頼ってばかりはいられない。
sentences.en: I can’t keep depending on my parents forever.

sentences.jp: 休んでばかりはいられない。仕事もしなくちゃ。
sentences.en: I can’t just keep taking breaks; I have work to do as well.

id: N2-～ではないか (〜de wa nai ka)
title: ～ではないか (〜de wa nai ka)
formation: Noun + ではないか / い-Adjective + ではないか / な-Adjective + ではないか / Verb-casual + ではないか
shortExplanation: Used to show a strong assertion or emphasis on one’s opinion; 'isn't it?', 'aren't you?', 'right?'.
longExplanation: The ～ではないか grammar point is used at the end of a sentence to make a rhetorical question or a strong, emphatic statement. It often translates as 'isn't it…?' 'aren't you…?' or 'wouldn’t you say…?'.

sentences.jp: 彼は優秀な学生ではないか。
sentences.en: Isn’t he an excellent student?

sentences.jp: この料理は美味しいではないか。
sentences.en: Isn’t this dish delicious?

sentences.jp: あなたは彼女の友達ではないか。
sentences.en: Aren’t you her friend?

sentences.jp: 明日は休みではないか。
sentences.en: Tomorrow’s a day off, right?

id: N2-～てはならない (〜te wa naranai)
title: ～てはならない (〜te wa naranai)
formation: Verb-て form + はならない
shortExplanation: Express prohibition; 'must not', 'cannot', 'should not'.
longExplanation: The ～てはならない grammar point is used to express a strong prohibition. It is often more formal or strict and can be translated as 'must not', 'cannot', or 'should not.'

sentences.jp: ここでタバコを吸ってはならない。
sentences.en: You must not smoke here.

sentences.jp: 秘密を他人に話してはならない。
sentences.en: You cannot reveal this secret to others.

sentences.jp: この部屋に入ってはならない。
sentences.en: You must not enter this room.

sentences.jp: 飲酒運転をしてはならない。
sentences.en: You must not drink and drive.

title: ～てまで (〜te made)
formation: Verb-て form + まで
shortExplanation: Express the extent someone goes in order to achieve something; 'to the point of', 'even', 'so far as to'.
longExplanation: The ～てまで grammar point is used to convey the extent someone goes to achieve something, highlighting the action one takes or the sacrifice one makes for a certain purpose. It can be translated as 'to the point of', 'even', or 'so far as to' in English. It is used with verbs, not adjectives or nouns.

sentences.jp: 彼は試験に合格するために、徹夜してまで勉強しました。
sentences.en: He studied to the point of staying up all night in order to pass the exam.

sentences.jp: この商品がほしかったので、朝早く起きてまで並んで買いました。
sentences.en: I wanted this product so much that I went so far as to get up early and wait in line to buy it.

sentences.jp: 彼女は彼を助けるために、お金を借りてまで手術代を払いました。
sentences.en: In order to help him, she even borrowed money to pay for the surgery.

sentences.jp: 部長はそのプロジェクトを成功させるために、休日も働いてまで取り組んでいます。
sentences.en: The manager is even working on holidays to make the project a success.

title: ～て当然だ (〜te tōzen da)
formation: Verb-て form + 当然だ
shortExplanation: Expresses that something is natural or expected.
longExplanation: The ～て当然だ grammar point is used to express that something is natural, obvious, or expected under the circumstances. It can be translated as 'it's natural that', 'as expected', or 'no wonder' in English.

sentences.jp: 彼は毎日勉強しているから、試験に合格して当然だ。
sentences.en: Since he studies every day, it's only natural that he passes the exam.

sentences.jp: そのレストランは料理が美味しいので、予約がいっぱいになって当然だ。
sentences.en: Because that restaurant’s food is delicious, no wonder its reservations are always full.

sentences.jp: 彼女は毎晩練習しているので、上手になって当然だ。
sentences.en: She practices every night, so it's natural that she becomes skilled.

sentences.jp: 彼は仕事で成功していて、お金持ちになって当然だ。
sentences.en: He has succeeded at his job, so it's only natural that he's become wealthy.

title: ～と～ともに (〜to 〜tomoni)
formation: Verb-casual + とともに, い-Adjective + とともに, な-Adjective + とともに, Noun + とともに
shortExplanation: Expresses 'together with' or 'along with'; used to describe two events or actions happening simultaneously or in parallel.
longExplanation: The ～と～ともに grammar point is used to express that two events or actions are happening simultaneously or in parallel. It is translated as 'together with' or 'along with' in English. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

sentences.jp: 彼が来るとともに、パーティーが始まりました。
sentences.en: The party started as soon as he arrived.

sentences.jp: 季節が変わるとともに、服のファッションも変わります。
sentences.en: As the seasons change, clothing fashion also changes.

sentences.jp: 成長とともに、いろいろな経験を積むことが大切です。
sentences.en: As you grow, it is important to gain various experiences.

sentences.jp: 技術の進歩とともに、便利なアプリが増えています。
sentences.en: With the advancement of technology, the number of convenient apps is increasing.

id: N2-～というものだ (〜to iu mono da)
title: ～というものだ (〜to iu mono da)
formation: Sentence + というものだ
shortExplanation: Expressing a general rule or common sense.
longExplanation: The ～というものだ grammar point is used to emphasize a general rule, a piece of common sense, or an indisputable fact. It often appears in comments or observations about something commonly accepted as true.

sentences.jp: 若者の熱情は尊敬に値するというものだ。
sentences.en: The passion of young people is something that deserves respect.

sentences.jp: 勉強は一日でもサボれば遅れるというものだ。
sentences.en: If you skip even a day of studying, you'll fall behind—that's just how it is.

sentences.jp: 成功するためには困難に立ち向かうというものだ。
sentences.en: To succeed, one must face difficulties—that's just the way it is.

sentences.jp: 友情はお金で買えないというものだ。
sentences.en: It is said that friendship cannot be bought with money.

title: ～とおり (〜toori)
formation: Verb-casual + とおり, い-Adjective + とおり, な-Adjective + だとおり, Noun + だとおり
shortExplanation: Express doing something as instructed or describing how something matches a rule or guideline; 'as', 'according to'.
longExplanation: The ～とおり grammar point is used to express doing something exactly as instructed or describing how something matches a guideline or is exactly as one heard/expected. It can be translated as 'as', 'according to', or 'the same way...'.

sentences.jp: 先生が言ったとおりに宿題をしました。
sentences.en: I did my homework exactly as the teacher said.

sentences.jp: この地図のとおりに歩いてください。
sentences.en: Please walk according to this map.

sentences.jp: 彼はいつもルールどおりに遊びます。
sentences.en: He always plays by the rules.

sentences.jp: その道路は昼間と同じくらい夜も賑やかだというとおりでした。
sentences.en: As I heard, that street was just as lively at night as it is in the daytime.

title: ～とか (〜to ka)
formation: Verb-casual + とか, い-Adjective + とか, な-Adjective + だとか, Noun + だとか
shortExplanation: Used to list examples or express uncertainty; 'such as', 'like', 'or something like that'.
longExplanation: The ～とか grammar point can be used to list examples or express a somewhat vague mention of items/activities. It can be translated as 'such as', 'like', or 'or something like that'.

sentences.jp: 休日は映画を見るとか、買い物に行くとかして過ごします。
sentences.en: On my days off, I do things like watch movies or go shopping.

sentences.jp: 彼女はオーストラリアとかニュージーランドとかに行きたがっています。
sentences.en: She wants to go to places like Australia or New Zealand.

sentences.jp: 今日の天気は晴れたり、曇ったりとかだそうです。
sentences.en: I heard today’s weather is something like sunny at times, cloudy at times.

sentences.jp: 彼は野球をしたり、サッカーをしたりとかしています。
sentences.en: He spends his time playing baseball and soccer and such.

title: ～ところ (〜tokoro)
formation: Verb-casual + ところ, い-Adjective + ところ, な-Adjective + なところ, Noun + のところ
shortExplanation: Express a specific point in time or state; 'when', 'just about', or 'in the middle of'.
longExplanation: The ～ところ grammar point is used to describe a specific point in time or stage of action. Depending on the verb tense, it can mean 'just about to do something', 'in the middle of doing something', or 'just did something'.

sentences.jp: 出かけるところです。
sentences.en: I'm just about to go out.

sentences.jp: 映画を見ているところです。
sentences.en: I'm in the middle of watching a movie.

sentences.jp: おいしいところで食事をするつもりです。
sentences.en: I plan to have a meal at a good (tasty) place.

sentences.jp: 彼は忙しいところを手伝ってくれました。
sentences.en: He helped me when he himself was busy.

title: ～どころか (〜dokoro ka)
formation: Verb-casual + どころか, い-Adjective + どころか, な-Adjective + どころか, Noun + どころか
shortExplanation: Express 'far from', 'not just', 'not only', or 'to say nothing of'.
longExplanation: The ～どころか grammar point is used to emphasize that reality is far from what one might expect or that something is not only one way, but actually goes beyond it. It can be translated as 'far from', 'not just', or 'to say nothing of'.

sentences.jp: 彼は歌が上手どころか、プロの歌手みたいだ。
sentences.en: Far from just being good at singing, he sounds like a professional singer.

sentences.jp: この料理はおいしいどころか、最高の味だ。
sentences.en: This dish is not just delicious, it’s absolutely the best.

sentences.jp: 彼女はただの友達どころか、私の親友だ。
sentences.en: She’s far from just a friend; she’s my best friend.

sentences.jp: 彼は英語ができるどころか、他の3つの言語も話せる。
sentences.en: Not only can he speak English, he also speaks three other languages.

id: N2-～どころではない (〜dokoro de wa nai)
title: ～どころではない (〜dokoro de wa nai)
formation: Verb-casual + どころではない, い-Adjective + どころではない, な-Adjective + どころではない, Noun + どころではない
shortExplanation: Not the time/place for; far from; can't possibly.
longExplanation: The ～どころではない grammar point is used to express that circumstances make it impossible or inappropriate to do something—there’s a much bigger issue at hand. It can also mean ‘far from’ in a figurative sense.

sentences.jp: 彼の失敗を笑うどころではない。私たちも同じミスをしている。
sentences.en: This is not the time to laugh at his mistakes—we’re making the same ones.

sentences.jp: 病気で寝ている時は、遊ぶどころではない。
sentences.en: When you’re sick in bed, it's far from the time to play around.

sentences.jp: 試験が近いので、映画を見るどころではない。
sentences.en: Since the exam is coming up, I can’t possibly watch a movie.

sentences.jp: 仕事が忙しすぎて、旅行なんて考えるどころではない。
sentences.en: I'm way too busy with work to even think about going on a trip.

title: ～としたら (〜to shitara)
formation: Verb-casual + としたら, い-Adjective + としたら, な-Adjective + だとしたら, Noun + だとしたら
shortExplanation: Express a hypothetical situation; 'if', 'suppose', 'assuming'.
longExplanation: The ～としたら grammar point is used to express a hypothetical situation or assumption. It can be translated as 'if', 'suppose', or 'assuming' in English. The formation differs depending on whether it’s used with verbs, adjectives, or nouns.

sentences.jp: 明日雨が降るとしたら、傘を持っていきましょう。
sentences.en: If it rains tomorrow, let’s bring umbrellas.

sentences.jp: このケーキが美味しくないとしたら、誰も食べないだろう。
sentences.en: If this cake isn’t tasty, probably no one will eat it.

sentences.jp: 彼が病気だとしたら、早めに病院へ行かないといけません。
sentences.en: If he is sick, he needs to go to the hospital soon.

sentences.jp: 彼女が学生だとしたら、このレストランは割引があるでしょう。
sentences.en: If she’s a student, there’s probably a discount at this restaurant.

title: ～としても (〜to shite mo)
formation: Verb-casual + としても, い-Adjective + としても, な-Adjective + だとしても, Noun + だとしても
shortExplanation: Even if, despite, although
longExplanation: The ～としても grammar point is used to say ‘even if...’ or ‘even assuming that...’, often followed by a conclusion or stance. It’s similar to ～ても, but puts more emphasis on the hypothetical assumption or viewpoint.

sentences.jp: 雨が降るとしても、レースは中止しません。
sentences.en: Even if it rains, we won't cancel the race.

sentences.jp: このケーキが美味しくないとしても、私は食べるつもりです。
sentences.en: Even if this cake isn't delicious, I plan on eating it.

sentences.jp: 彼女が遅刻するとしても、私たちは待ち続けます。
sentences.en: Even if she is late, we will keep waiting.

sentences.jp: 忙しいだとしても、友達との約束は守りたい。
sentences.en: Even if I'm busy, I want to keep my promise to my friend.

id: N2-～とは限らない (〜to wa kagiranai)
title: ～とは限らない (〜to wa kagiranai)
formation: Verb-casual + とは限らない, い-Adjective + とは限らない, な-Adjective + だとは限らない, Noun + だとは限らない
shortExplanation: Expresses that something is not necessarily true; 'not always', 'not necessarily'.
longExplanation: The ～とは限らない grammar point is used to express that something is not necessarily true or does not always occur. It can be translated as 'not always' or 'not necessarily' in English. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

sentences.jp: いつも勉強しているとは限らない。
sentences.en: It's not always true that I'm studying.

sentences.jp: 高い商品がいいとは限らない。
sentences.en: Expensive products are not necessarily good.

sentences.jp: 彼が正しいとは限らない。
sentences.en: It's not necessarily true that he is right.

sentences.jp: 雨が降るとは限らない。
sentences.en: It's not necessarily going to rain.

id: N2-～ないことはない (〜nai koto wa nai)
title: ～ないことはない (〜nai koto wa nai)
formation: Verb-ないform + ことはない
shortExplanation: Express that something is not impossible, meaning it is possible or could be done.
longExplanation: The ～ないことはない grammar point is used to express a double negative, meaning that something is not impossible, implying that it is possible or could be done. Essentially, it downplays the possibility or ability to do something but still acknowledges that it's possible.

sentences.jp: 彼が来ないことはない。
sentences.en: It's not that he won't come (it's possible that he will come).

sentences.jp: あの問題が解けないことはない。
sentences.en: It's not impossible to solve that problem (it can be solved).

sentences.jp: その仕事が難しくないことはない。
sentences.en: That job is not without difficulty (it has some difficulties).

sentences.jp: 彼女が正しくないことはない。
sentences.en: It's not impossible that she is right (she could be right).

id: N2-～ないこともない (〜nai koto mo nai)
title: ～ないこともない (〜nai koto mo nai)
formation: Verb-ないForm + こともない, い-adjective + こともない
shortExplanation: Used to express an action or state is 'not impossible', but emphasizes reluctance or doubt.
longExplanation: The ～ないこともない grammar point is used to express that something is not impossible, but the speaker is reluctant, has doubts, or deems it as a less favorable option. It can be translated as 'not that one can't', 'not that it's impossible', or 'it's not out of the question'.

sentences.jp: この問題は解けないこともないけど、難しいです。
sentences.en: It's not that I can't solve this problem, but it's difficult.

sentences.jp: 彼に会わないこともないが、あまり話す時間がない。
sentences.en: It's not that I can't meet him, but there's not much time to talk.

sentences.jp: お金がないこともないけれど、節約した方がいい。
sentences.en: It's not that I don't have money, but it's better to save.

sentences.jp: このゲームが面白くないこともないが、他のゲームの方が楽しい。
sentences.en: It's not that this game isn't interesting, but other games are more fun.

id: N2-～ないではいられない (〜nai de wa irarenai)
title: ～ないではいられない (〜nai de wa irarenai)
formation: Verb-ない form + ではいられない or じゃいられない
shortExplanation: Cannot help but; cannot avoid; unable to refrain from.
longExplanation: The ～ないではいられない grammar point is used to express a strong feeling, urge, or uncontrollable action that someone cannot resist or help doing, even if it may be undesirable. It can be translated as 'cannot help but', 'cannot avoid', or 'unable to refrain from' in English.

sentences.jp: この映画はとても感動的で、泣かないではいられなかった。
sentences.en: This movie was so touching, I couldn't help but cry.

sentences.jp: 彼女の笑顔を見ると、笑わないではいられない。
sentences.en: When I see her smile, I can't help but laugh.

sentences.jp: この部屋はとても寒いので、震えないではいられない。
sentences.en: This room is so cold, I can't help but shiver.

sentences.jp: 彼の話はつまらないが、聞かないではいられない。
sentences.en: His story is boring, but I can't help but listen.

title: ～ながら (〜nagara)
formation: Verb-stem + ながら
shortExplanation: Express doing two things simultaneously or at the same time.
longExplanation: The ～ながら grammar point is used to express the concept of doing two things simultaneously or at the same time. In English, it can be translated as 'while', 'as', or 'during'. ～ながら can be attached to the stem of a verb to convey this meaning.

sentences.jp: テレビを見ながら宿題をしています。
sentences.en: I am doing my homework while watching TV.

sentences.jp: 彼女は歌いながら料理を作っていました。
sentences.en: She was cooking while singing.

sentences.jp: 電車の中で立ちながら本を読んでいる人が多いです。
sentences.en: There are many people who read books while standing on the train.

sentences.jp: 僕は歩きながらスマホを使っています。
sentences.en: I am using my smartphone while walking.

title: ～にあたり (〜ni atari)
formation: Noun + にあたり
shortExplanation: Expresses the meaning of 'at the time of', 'in', 'on', or 'upon'.
longExplanation: The ～にあたり grammar point is used to describe an important event or situation that occurs at a specific moment. It can be translated as 'at the time of', 'in', 'on', or 'upon' in English. The formation is quite simple, as it only requires a noun before it.

sentences.jp: 入学にあたり、必要な書類を準備してください。
sentences.en: Please prepare the necessary documents upon entering the school.

sentences.jp: 新年にあたり、抱負を立てる人が多いです。
sentences.en: Many people set goals at the time of the new year.

sentences.jp: 結婚にあたり、両家は大切な儀式を行います。
sentences.en: Upon getting married, both families conduct an important ceremony.

sentences.jp: 新しい仕事を始めるにあたり、準備が必要です。
sentences.en: In starting a new job, preparation is necessary.

title: ～において (〜ni oite)
formation: Noun + において
shortExplanation: Indicates the place, time, or context where an action or event occurs; 'in', 'at', 'on', 'regarding'.
longExplanation: The ～において grammar point is used to show the place, time, or context where an action or event takes place. It can be translated as 'in', 'at', 'on', or 'regarding' in English. It is often used in formal situations and can be replaced by more casual equivalents such as で, に, or は in informal contexts.

sentences.jp: この大学において、英語のクラスが一番人気があります。
sentences.en: In this university, English classes are the most popular.

sentences.jp: 彼の研究において新しい発見がありました。
sentences.en: In his research, there were new discoveries.

sentences.jp: 運動会において、彼は大活躍しました。
sentences.en: At the sports festival, he performed exceptionally well.

sentences.jp: 環境問題においては、みんなが責任を持って行動すべきです。
sentences.en: Regarding environmental issues, everyone should take responsibility and act accordingly.

title: ～にかかわらず (〜ni kakawarazu)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + にかかわらず, い-Adjective + にかかわらず, な-Adjective + にかかわらず, Noun + にかかわらず
shortExplanation: Regardless of; irrespective of; (often used as) 'whether or not'.
longExplanation: The ～にかかわらず grammar point is used to express that something happens or is true regardless of a particular fact, condition, or circumstance. It can be translated as 'regardless of', 'irrespective of', or 'whether or not'. It can be used with verbs, い-adjectives, な-adjectives, or nouns.

sentences.jp: 天気にかかわらず、今日の試合は行われます。
sentences.en: The game today will be held, regardless of the weather.

sentences.jp: 彼は年齢にかかわらず、新しいことを学び続けています。
sentences.en: He continues to learn new things, regardless of his age.

sentences.jp: その仕事が難しいかどうかにかかわらず、彼女は努力し続けた。
sentences.en: Regardless of whether the job is difficult or not, she continued to make an effort.

sentences.jp: 経験の有無にかかわらず、このプログラムは誰でも利用できます。
sentences.en: Regardless of experience, this program can be used by anyone.

title: ～にかけては (〜ni kakete wa)
formation: Noun + にかけては
shortExplanation: Indicates a specific area or aspect in which someone or something excels.
longExplanation: The grammar point ～にかけては is used to emphasize a particular area or aspect in which someone or something excels or is exceptional. This structure is often used with positive connotations regarding the subject's expertise or outstanding qualities.

sentences.jp: 料理の腕前にかけては、彼女は誰にも負けません。
sentences.en: When it comes to cooking skills, she is second to none.

sentences.jp: この町にかけては、彼の店が一番おいしいラーメンを売っています。
sentences.en: As far as this town is concerned, his store sells the most delicious ramen.

sentences.jp: 音楽の知識にかけては、彼は私たちの中で一番詳しいです。
sentences.en: When it comes to music knowledge, he is the most knowledgeable among us.

sentences.jp: スポーツにかけては、彼はどんな競技も得意です。
sentences.en: When it comes to sports, he excels in any competition.

title: ～にしたがって (〜ni shitagatte)
formation: Noun + にしたがって
shortExplanation: Expresses a natural consequence or a change that occurs according to something else; 'as', 'with', 'in accordance with'.
longExplanation: The ～にしたがって grammar point is used to express a natural consequence or a change that occurs according to something else. It can be translated as 'as', 'with', or 'in accordance with' in English. This grammar point is used with nouns and can be used to show a change in proportion or in accordance with a rule or principle.

sentences.jp: 成長にしたがって、この植物の葉が大きくなります。
sentences.en: As it grows, the leaves of this plant become larger.

sentences.jp: 説明書にしたがって、家具を組み立ててください。
sentences.en: Please assemble the furniture according to the instructions.

sentences.jp: 年齢にしたがって、人の体力は低下します。
sentences.en: As people age, their physical strength declines.

sentences.jp: 運動にしたがって、心拍数が増加することが一般的です。
sentences.en: It is common for the heart rate to increase with exercise.

title: ～にしたら (〜ni shitara)
formation: Noun + にしたら, Pronoun + にしたら
shortExplanation: Express a point of view; 'from the perspective of', 'if I were'.
longExplanation: The ～にしたら grammar point is used to express a point of view or opinion from someone else's perspective. It can be translated as 'from the perspective of' or 'if I were' in English. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a noun or a pronoun.

sentences.jp: 彼にしたら、早く帰りたいだろう。
sentences.en: From his perspective, he probably wants to go home early.

sentences.jp: 子供たちにしたら、この話はつまらないかもしれない。
sentences.en: If I were the children, this story might be boring.

sentences.jp: 私にしたら、そのプレゼントは素晴らしいと思います。
sentences.en: From my perspective, I think that present is wonderful.

sentences.jp: 彼女にしたら、この映画が好きではないでしょう。
sentences.en: If I were her, I would not like this movie.

id: N2-～にしろ～にしろ (〜ni shiro 〜ni shiro)
title: ～にしろ～にしろ (〜ni shiro 〜ni shiro)
formation: Verb (dictionary form or negative form) + にしろ, い-Adjective + にしろ, な-Adjective + にしろ, Noun + にしろ
shortExplanation: Expresses 'either way', 'in any case', 'be it...', used when presenting two alternatives.
longExplanation: The ～にしろ～にしろ grammar point is used to present two alternatives and emphasize that regardless of which one is chosen or occurs, the result or conclusion remains the same. It can be translated as 'either way', 'in any case', or 'be it...'. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

sentences.jp: 行くにしろ行かないにしろ、早く決めてください。
sentences.en: Either way, whether you go or not, please decide quickly.

sentences.jp: 彼が来るにしろ来ないにしろ、パーティーは始めましょう。
sentences.en: Be it if he comes or not, let's start the party either way.

sentences.jp: 雨が降るにしろ降らないにしろ、傘を持って行ったほうがいいです。
sentences.en: Either way, whether it rains or not, it's better to bring an umbrella.

sentences.jp: このレストランが高いにしろ安いにしろ、料理は美味しいです。
sentences.en: In any case, whether this restaurant is expensive or cheap, the food is delicious.

id: N2-～につけ～につけ (〜ni tsuke 〜ni tsuke)
title: ～につけ～につけ (〜ni tsuke 〜ni tsuke)
formation: Verb-casual + につけ, い-Adjective + につけ, な-Adjective + につけ, Noun + につけ
shortExplanation: Expresses 'whenever', 'each time', 'whether...or'.
longExplanation: The ～につけ～につけ grammar point is used to express that something happens 'each time' or 'whenever' a certain event or situation occurs, and can also mean 'whether...or'. This grammar point indicates that the same event or feeling occurs no matter which of the following situations happen.

sentences.jp: 外国へ行くにつけ、勉強するにつけ、言葉の壁にぶつかる。
sentences.en: Whether I go abroad or study, I always encounter language barriers.

sentences.jp: 彼と話すにつけ、彼が考えるにつけ、彼の知識に驚かされる。
sentences.en: Whenever I talk to him or hear his thoughts, I'm always amazed by his knowledge.

sentences.jp: この公園で遊ぶにつけ、散歩するにつけ、子供時代を思い出す。
sentences.en: Each time I play or take a walk in this park, it reminds me of my childhood.

sentences.jp: 春につけ、秋につけ、彼は母校を訪れる。
sentences.en: Whether in spring or autumn, he visits his alma mater.

title: ～につれて (〜ni tsurete)
formation: Verb (dictionary form) + につれて, Noun + につれて
shortExplanation: Expresses the idea that as one thing changes, so does another; 'as', 'along with', 'while'.
longExplanation: The ～につれて grammar point is used to express the idea that as one thing changes or progresses, another thing changes or progresses simultaneously. In English, it can be translated as 'as', 'along with', or 'while'.

sentences.jp: 年を取るにつれて、健康に気をつけなければなりません。
sentences.en: As you get older, you have to pay more attention to your health.

sentences.jp: 春が近づくにつれて、気温が上がる。
sentences.en: As spring approaches, the temperature rises.

sentences.jp: 勉強するにつれて、だんだん分かるようになる。
sentences.en: As I study, I gradually come to understand.

sentences.jp: 経験が増えるにつれて、自信もついてくる。
sentences.en: As your experience increases, so does your confidence.

title: ～にともなって (〜ni tomonatte)
formation: Noun (event) + にともなって, Verb-casual + にともなって
shortExplanation: Expresses 'along with' or 'as a result of'; indicates that one event occurs along with another.
longExplanation: The ～にともなって grammar point is used to indicate that one event occurs along with another event, or as a result of another event. It can be translated as 'along with', 'as a result of', or 'in accordance with' in English.

sentences.jp: 人口が増えるにともなって、交通渋滞も悪化しています。
sentences.en: As the population increases, traffic congestion is also getting worse.

sentences.jp: 季節の変わり目にともなって、体調を崩しやすくなります。
sentences.en: As the seasons change, it's easier to get out of shape.

sentences.jp: 経済の成長にともなって、雇用の機会も増えるでしょう。
sentences.en: As the economy grows, employment opportunities will also increase.

sentences.jp: 彼の昇進にともなって、引っ越しをすることになりました。
sentences.en: As a result of his promotion, we decided to move.

id: N2-～にほかならない (〜ni hoka naranai)
title: ～にほかならない (〜ni hoka naranai)
formation: Verb-plain + にほかならない, い-Adjective + にほかならない, な-Adjective + にほかならない, Noun + にほかならない
shortExplanation: Express that something is 'nothing but' or 'none other than'.
longExplanation: The ～にほかならない grammar point is used to emphasize that something is no more or no less than what is specified. It can be seen as a strong way to say that something is 'nothing but' or 'none other than'.

sentences.jp: 彼の成功は努力にほかならない。
sentences.en: His success is nothing but hard work.

sentences.jp: この痛みは寒さにほかならない。
sentences.en: This pain is nothing but the cold.

sentences.jp: 彼の失敗の原因は慢心にほかならない。
sentences.en: The cause of his failure is nothing but overconfidence.

sentences.jp: 彼女の笑顔は明るさにほかならない。
sentences.en: Her smile is none other than brightness.

id: N2-～にもかかわらず (〜ni mo kakawarazu)
title: ～にもかかわらず (〜ni mo kakawarazu)
formation: Verb (any tense/form) + にもかかわらず, い-Adjective + にもかかわらず, な-Adjective (or Noun) + であるにもかかわらず
shortExplanation: Expresses 'despite', 'in spite of' or 'even though'.
longExplanation: The ～にもかかわらず grammar point is used to express that something occurs, or a situation exists, despite or in spite of a contrasting or unfavorable condition. It can be translated as 'despite', 'in spite of', or 'even though' in English.

sentences.jp: 忙しいにもかかわらず、彼は毎日運動しています。
sentences.en: Despite being busy, he exercises every day.

sentences.jp: 雨が降っているにもかかわらず、彼女は散歩に行きました。
sentences.en: Even though it was raining, she went for a walk.

sentences.jp: 車が古いにもかかわらず、まだよく走ります。
sentences.en: In spite of the car being old, it still runs well.

sentences.jp: 結果が良くないにもかかわらず、彼は頑張り続けました。
sentences.en: Despite the results not being good, he continued to do his best.

title: ～により (〜ni yori)
formation: Noun + により, Verb + により
shortExplanation: Indicates the reason, method, or means by which something happens; 'by', 'through', 'due to'.
longExplanation: The ～により grammar point is used to indicate the reason, method, or means by which something happens or is done. It can be often translated as 'by', 'through', or 'due to' in English. It can be attached directly to nouns or verbs in plain form.

sentences.jp: この事故は運転手の不注意により起こりました。
sentences.en: This accident occurred due to the driver's carelessness.

sentences.jp: その映画は小説により制作されました。
sentences.en: The movie was produced based on the novel.

sentences.jp: 犯人は警察により逮捕されました。
sentences.en: The criminal was arrested by the police.

sentences.jp: 会社の成長は従業員の努力により実現しました。
sentences.en: The company's growth was achieved through the efforts of its employees.

title: ～にわたって (〜ni watatte)
formation: Noun + にわたって
shortExplanation: Indicates a range or extent; 'over', 'throughout', 'across'.
longExplanation: The ～にわたって grammar point is used to express a range or extent of a particular action, event, or situation. It can be translated as 'over', 'throughout', or 'across' in English. This structure can be used with various types of words such as time, distance, and quantity.

sentences.jp: 彼女は一年にわたって日本語を勉強しました。
sentences.en: She studied Japanese for over a year.

sentences.jp: このプロジェクトは50キロにわたって道路を建設します。
sentences.en: This project will construct roads across 50 kilometers.

sentences.jp: 彼の著作は数多くの国にわたって読まれている。
sentences.en: His writings are read throughout numerous countries.

sentences.jp: その秘密は世代にわたって保たれ続けた。
sentences.en: The secret was kept over generations.

title: ～に先立ち (〜ni sakidachi)
formation: Noun + に先立ち / Verb-dictionary form + に先立って
shortExplanation: Express an action or event that occurs before another; 'before', 'prior to', 'preceding'.
longExplanation: The ～に先立ち grammar point is used to indicate that a certain action or event occurs before another action or event. It can be translated as 'before', 'prior to', or 'preceding' in English. It is often used with nouns (Noun + に先立ち) or with dictionary-form verbs (Verb-dictionary form + に先立って).

sentences.jp: 会議に先立ち、資料を確認しましょう。
sentences.en: Before the meeting, let's check the materials.

sentences.jp: 出発に先立ち、荷物を整理しました。
sentences.en: Before departure, I organized my luggage.

sentences.jp: 就寝に先立ち、歯を磨きます。
sentences.en: Before going to bed, I brush my teeth.

sentences.jp: 映画の上映に先立ち、予告編が放映されます。
sentences.en: Before the movie screening, the trailers are shown.

title: ～に反して (〜ni hanshite)
formation: Noun + に反して
shortExplanation: Contrary to, against, in spite of
longExplanation: The ～に反して grammar point is used to express that something is contrary to or against an expectation or belief. It can be translated as 'contrary to', 'against', or 'in spite of' in English. It is generally used with nouns.

sentences.jp: 天気予報に反して、今日は雨が降っている。
sentences.en: Contrary to the weather forecast, it is raining today.

sentences.jp: 彼の予想に反して、試合に勝った。
sentences.en: We won the match against his prediction.

sentences.jp: 両親の意見に反して、彼と結婚した。
sentences.en: I married him against my parents' opinion.

sentences.jp: この映画は批評家の評価に反して大ヒットした。
sentences.en: This movie became a big hit contrary to the critics' ratings.

title: ～に基づいて (〜ni motozuite)
formation: Noun + に基づいて
shortExplanation: Based on, according to, depending on
longExplanation: The ～に基づいて grammar point is used to express that something is based on, according to, or depending on a certain source or foundation. It can be used with nouns that represent a source, rule, principle, or standard.

sentences.jp: 研究結果に基づいて、新しい治療法が開発されました。
sentences.en: A new treatment method was developed based on the research results.

sentences.jp: 法律に基づいて、犯罪者は逮捕されました。
sentences.en: The criminal was arrested according to the law.

sentences.jp: そのデータに基づいて、結論を出すことができます。
sentences.en: We can draw conclusions based on that data.

sentences.jp: お客様の要望に基づいて、商品の改善が行われます。
sentences.en: Product improvements will be made based on customer requests.

title: ～に対して (〜ni taishite)
formation: Noun + に対して
shortExplanation: Expresses 'toward', 'against', 'in contrast to', or 'in response to'.
longExplanation: The ～に対して grammar point is used to indicate a direction or target, showing an action or attitude towards something or someone. It can also be used to show a contrast between two situations or to express 'in response to' an action or situation.

sentences.jp: 日本の文化に対して尊敬の念を持っています。
sentences.en: I have a sense of respect towards Japanese culture.

sentences.jp: 彼に対して無礼な態度を取らないでください。
sentences.en: Please don't have a rude attitude towards him.

sentences.jp: 先生の質問に対して、彼女はすぐに答えました。
sentences.en: In response to the teacher's question, she answered immediately.

sentences.jp: 男性に対して、女性は同じ仕事でも低い給料をもらっている。
sentences.en: In contrast to men, women receive lower salaries for the same job.

title: ～に応えて (〜ni kotaete)
formation: Noun + に応えて
shortExplanation: Responding to; living up to; meeting.
longExplanation: The ～に応えて grammar point is used to express an action in response to something (an expectation, request, or situation). It can be translated as 'responding to', 'living up to', or 'meeting' in English. This grammar point is generally used with nouns or noun phrases.

sentences.jp: お客様の要望に応えて、新しいメニューを作りました。
sentences.en: Responding to the customers' requests, we created a new menu.

sentences.jp: 彼はチームの期待に応えて素晴らしいプレーを見せた。
sentences.en: He lived up to the team's expectations and showed a great performance.

sentences.jp: 政府は国民の声に応えて、新しい法律を制定した。
sentences.en: The government enacted a new law in response to the voice of the people.

sentences.jp: 企業は環境問題に応えて、環境に優しい製品を開発している。
sentences.en: Companies are developing environmentally friendly products in response to environmental issues.

title: ～に応じて (〜ni oujite)
formation: Noun + に応じて
shortExplanation: In accordance with, depending on, in response to.
longExplanation: The ～に応じて grammar point is used to express something that varies according to a certain condition or responds to a change. It can be translated as 'in accordance with', 'depending on', or 'in response to' in English. The formation is used with nouns.

sentences.jp: 仕事の量に応じて給料が変わります。
sentences.en: The salary changes depending on the amount of work.

sentences.jp: 季節に応じて服装を選びましょう。
sentences.en: Choose your clothes according to the season.

sentences.jp: お客様の希望に応じてプランを提案します。
sentences.en: We propose plans according to the customers' wishes.

sentences.jp: 年齢に応じて健康診断項目が異なります。
sentences.en: Health check items vary depending on age.

id: N2-～に決まっている (〜ni kimatte iru)
title: ～に決まっている (〜ni kimatte iru)
formation: Verb-casual + に決まっている, い-Adjective + に決まっている, な-Adjective + だに決まっている, Noun + だに決まっている
shortExplanation: Express certainty; 'it is obvious', 'it must be', 'no doubt'.
longExplanation: The ～に決まっている grammar point is used to express certainty or a strong conviction about a situation or event. It can be translated in English as 'it is obvious', 'it must be', or 'no doubt'. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

sentences.jp: こんなに暑い日に運動したら、汗をかくに決まっている。
sentences.en: It's obvious that you'll sweat if you exercise on such a hot day.

sentences.jp: 彼が試験に落ちたら、がっかりするに決まっている。
sentences.en: If he fails the exam, it's obvious he will be disappointed.

sentences.jp: 彼女はいつも遅れるから、今日も遅れるに決まっている。
sentences.en: She's always late, so it's obvious she'll be late today too.

sentences.jp: このゲームは楽しいに決まっている。
sentences.en: It's obvious that this game is fun.

title: ～に沿って (〜ni sotte)
formation: Noun + に沿って
shortExplanation: Along with; in accordance with; following
longExplanation: The ～に沿って grammar point is used to express that something happens or is done along with, in accordance with, or following a particular line, path, or guideline. It is often used in instructions or explanations. The に沿って is attached to the noun representing the line, path or guideline.

sentences.jp: 川に沿って歩いていくと、橋が見えてきます。
sentences.en: If you walk along the river, you will see a bridge.

sentences.jp: このレシピに沿って料理を作れば、間違いありません。
sentences.en: If you cook according to this recipe, there will be no mistakes.

sentences.jp: 規則に沿って行動しなければなりません。
sentences.en: We must act in accordance with the rules.

sentences.jp: 山道に沿って進むと、小さな神社があります。
sentences.en: If you follow the mountain path, there is a small shrine.

title: ～に過ぎない (〜ni suginai)
formation: Verb-casual + に過ぎない, い-Adjective + に過ぎない, な-Adjective + に過ぎない, Noun + に過ぎない
shortExplanation: Expresses that something is 'just' or 'nothing more than'; 'merely', 'only', 'simply'.
longExplanation: The ～に過ぎない grammar point is used to indicate that something is 'just' or 'nothing more than' the stated quality or condition. It implies that the thing is not as significant or important as it might seem at first glance. This grammar point can be translated as 'merely', 'only', or 'simply' in English.
