It can be used with verbs, い-adjectives, な-adjectives, and nouns.

sentences.jp: 彼の失敗はミスに過ぎない。
sentences.en: His failure is nothing more than a mistake.

sentences.jp: この試験は簡単な問題に過ぎない。
sentences.en: This test is nothing more than a simple question.

sentences.jp: 彼女は友達に過ぎない。
sentences.en: She is nothing more than a friend.

sentences.jp: その発言は冗談に過ぎない。
sentences.en: That remark is nothing more than a joke.

title: ～に関わって (〜ni kakawatte)
formation: Verb-casual + に関わって, い-Adjective + に関わって, な-Adjective + に関わって, Noun + に関わって
shortExplanation: Indicates that something is related to or influenced by a certain matter or factor; 'concerning', 'in connection with', 'regarding'.
longExplanation: The ～に関わって grammar point is used to express that something is related to, influenced by, or connected with a certain matter or factor. It can be translated as 'concerning', 'in connection with', or 'regarding' in English. The formation depends on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

sentences.jp: 試験の内容に関わって、先生に質問があります。
sentences.en: I have a question for the teacher regarding the content of the exam.

sentences.jp: このプロジェクトに関わって、意見があれば、遠慮なく言ってください。
sentences.en: If you have any opinions regarding this project, please feel free to express them.

sentences.jp: 健康に関わって、食生活はとても重要です。
sentences.en: Regarding health, dietary habits are very important.

sentences.jp: その事件に関わって、彼も少し疑われています。
sentences.en: He is also somewhat suspected in connection with the incident.

title: ～に限り (〜ni kagiri)
formation: Noun + に限り
shortExplanation: Expresses limitation; 'only', 'limited to', 'exclusive to'.
longExplanation: The ～に限り grammar point is used to indicate that something is limited or exclusive to a certain condition or situation. It can be translated as 'only', 'limited to', or 'exclusive to' in English. The formation is simple and can be used with nouns.

sentences.jp: 今日に限り、この商品が半額です。
sentences.en: Today only, this product is half price.

sentences.jp: このクーポンはこの店に限り有効です。
sentences.en: This coupon is valid only at this store.

sentences.jp: 会員に限り、無料でこのサービスを利用できます。
sentences.en: Only members can use this service for free.

sentences.jp: この特典は先着10名に限ります。
sentences.en: This benefit is limited to the first 10 people.

title: ～に際して (〜ni saishite)
formation: Noun + に際して
shortExplanation: Used to express the moment or the occasion of an action; 'at the time of', 'upon', 'when'.
longExplanation: The ～に際して grammar point describes an action that occurs during a specific event, occasion, or when something takes place. It can be translated into English as 'at the time of', 'upon', 'when', or 'in the event of'. This grammar is used with nouns to describe the context or situation during which an action takes place.

sentences.jp: 卒業式に際して、校長先生がスピーチをします。
sentences.en: At the graduation ceremony, the principal gives a speech.

sentences.jp: この仕事に際して、責任感が大切です。
sentences.en: Responsibility is important when doing this job.

sentences.jp: 留学に際して、ビザの手続きが必要です。
sentences.en: Visa procedures are necessary when studying abroad.

sentences.jp: 結婚式に際して、両親に感謝の気持ちを伝えました。
sentences.en: At the time of the wedding ceremony, I conveyed my gratitude to my parents.

title: ～ねばならない (〜neba naranai)
formation: Verb-ない form → (remove い) → ね + ばならない
shortExplanation: Expresses obligation or necessity; 'must', 'have to', 'need to'.
longExplanation: The ～ねばならない grammar point is used to express obligation or necessity. It can be translated as 'must', 'have to', or 'need to' in English. This grammar point is mainly used with verbs, indicating that the action must be done.

sentences.jp: 試験に合格するために、もっと勉強せねばならない。
sentences.en: To pass the exam, I must study more.

sentences.jp: 飛行機に間に合うためには、急がねばならない。
sentences.en: To catch the flight, I must hurry.

sentences.jp: ６時までに帰らねばならない。
sentences.en: I have to go home by 6 o'clock.

sentences.jp: この仕事を今日中に終わらせねばならない。
sentences.en: I need to finish this work by the end of the day.

title: ～のみならず～も (〜nomi narazu 〜mo)
formation: Verb-casual + のみならず + (も), い-Adjective + のみならず + (も), な-Adjective + のみならず + (も), Noun + のみならず + (も)
shortExplanation: Not only... but also; as well as.
longExplanation: The ～のみならず～も grammar point is used to show that something applies not only to one thing, but also to another thing. This can be translated as 'not only... but also', or 'as well as' in English. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

sentences.jp: 彼は英語のみならず、フランス語も話せます。
sentences.en: He can speak not only English but also French.

sentences.jp: このレストランは美味しいのみならず、雰囲気も素晴らしいです。
sentences.en: Not only is this restaurant delicious, but the atmosphere is also great.

sentences.jp: このアプリは便利のみならず、安全性も高いです。
sentences.en: This app is not only convenient but also highly secure.

sentences.jp: 彼女は頭が良いのみならず、スポーツも得意です。
sentences.en: She is not only smart but also good at sports.

id: N2-～のももっともだ (〜no mo mottomo da)
title: ～のももっともだ (〜no mo mottomo da)
formation: Verb-casual + のももっともだ, い-Adjective + のももっともだ, な-Adjective + なのももっともだ, Noun + なのももっともだ
shortExplanation: Express that something is understandable or reasonable.
longExplanation: The ～のももっともだ grammar point is used to express that a situation, action, or feeling is understandable or reasonable, considering the circumstances. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

sentences.jp: 彼が緊張するのももっともだ。
sentences.en: It's understandable that he is nervous.

sentences.jp: この問題が難しいのももっともだ。
sentences.en: It's reasonable that this problem is difficult.

sentences.jp: 彼女が幸せなのももっともだ。
sentences.en: It's understandable that she is happy.

sentences.jp: 彼がリーダーなのももっともだ。
sentences.en: It's reasonable that he is the leader.

title: ～の下で (〜no shita de)
formation: Verb-casual + の下で, い-Adjective + の下で, な-Adjective + の下で, Noun + の下で
shortExplanation: Under the context or condition of; under.
longExplanation: The ～の下で grammar point is used to express that something occurs under a certain context, condition, or circumstance. It can be translated as 'under' or 'in' in English. The formation is simple, as it can be used with verbs, い-adjectives, な-adjectives, and nouns.

sentences.jp: 先生の下で勉強するのはとても楽しいです。
sentences.en: Studying under the teacher is very enjoyable.

sentences.jp: 晴天の下でピクニックをしましょう。
sentences.en: Let's have a picnic under the clear sky.

sentences.jp: 緊張感の下で、彼女はスピーチに成功しました。
sentences.en: Under pressure, she succeeded in her speech.

sentences.jp: 親の愛情の下で、子供たちは幸せに育ちます。
sentences.en: Under the love of their parents, children grow up happily.

id: N2-～ば～というものでもない (〜ba 〜to iu mono demo nai)
title: ～ば～というものでもない (〜ba 〜to iu mono demo nai)
formation: Verb-ば form + というものでもない
shortExplanation: Not necessarily true that if A, then B.
longExplanation: The ～ば～というものでもない grammar point is used to express that something is not necessarily true even if a certain condition is met. In other words, if A happens, it doesn't always mean B will happen.

sentences.jp: 高いものがいいものばかりではない。
sentences.en: Expensive things are not always good.

sentences.jp: 英語ができれば、成功するというものでもない。
sentences.en: Just because you can speak English doesn’t mean you’ll succeed.

sentences.jp: 見た目が美しいからといって、心も美しいというものでもない。
sentences.en: Just because someone is beautiful on the outside, it doesn’t mean they’re beautiful on the inside.

sentences.jp: 有名大学に入れば、将来が安泰だというものでもない。
sentences.en: Getting into a famous university doesn’t guarantee a secure future.

title: ～ばかりか〜も (〜bakari ka 〜 mo)
formation: Noun/Verb/Adjective + ばかりか + Noun/Verb/Adjective + も
shortExplanation: Expresses 'not only... but also'; emphasizing the extent of something.
longExplanation: The ～ばかりか〜も grammar point is used to express the idea of 'not only... but also' in a sentence. It emphasizes the extent to which something applies, showing a certain component or factor is not the only relevant one.

sentences.jp: 彼はかわいいばかりか、賢くもある。
sentences.en: He is not only cute but also smart.

sentences.jp: このレストランは味が良いばかりかサービスも素晴らしいです。
sentences.en: This restaurant not only has great taste but also wonderful service.

sentences.jp: 彼女は日本語が上手ばかりか英語も話せる。
sentences.en: She can speak not only Japanese but also English.

sentences.jp: 彼は歌が上手いばかりかダンスもうまい。
sentences.en: He is not only good at singing but also dancing.

title: ～ばかりだ (〜bakari da)
formation: Verb-てform + ばかりだ, い-Adjective + ばかりだ, な-Adjective + ばかりだ, Noun + ばかりだ
shortExplanation: Express a continuing state or negative tendency; 'just', 'only', 'always'.
longExplanation: The ～ばかりだ grammar point is used to express a continuing or unchanging state, often emphasizing a negative or undesired tendency. It can be translated as 'just', 'only', or 'always' in English. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

sentences.jp: 彼は文句を言ってばかりだ。
sentences.en: He is always complaining.

sentences.jp: この映画は面白くなくて、退屈してばかりだ。
sentences.en: This movie isn't interesting, and I'm just bored.

sentences.jp: 彼女は暇でいるばかりだ。
sentences.en: She is always just idling around with nothing to do.

sentences.jp: 最近、仕事の失敗ばかりだ。
sentences.en: Lately, it’s just been one failure at work after another.

title: ～ばかりに (〜bakari ni)
formation: Verb-casual + ばかりに, い-Adjective + ばかりに, な-Adjective + なばかりに, Noun + なばかりに
shortExplanation: Express an undesired situation or result caused by something; 'simply because', 'due to', 'on account of'.
longExplanation: The ～ばかりに grammar point is used to express an undesired situation or result that arises simply because of some factor. It can be translated as 'simply because', 'due to', or 'on account of' in English. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

sentences.jp: 雨が降ったばかりに、試合が中止になりました。
sentences.en: Simply because it rained, the match was cancelled.

sentences.jp: 彼が遅れたばかりに、私たちも遅刻しました。
sentences.en: Because he was late, we ended up being late too.

sentences.jp: 忘れ物をしたばかりに、買い物に戻らなければなりません。
sentences.en: Simply because I forgot something, I have to go back to the store.

sentences.jp: 彼女が美人なばかりに、みんなが彼女を羨んでいます。
sentences.en: Just because she is beautiful, everyone is envious of her.

title: ～はともかく～は (〜wa tomokaku 〜wa)
formation: Noun1 はともかく Noun2 は
shortExplanation: Used to compare or contrast two elements; 'as for...'.
longExplanation: The grammar point ～はともかく～は is used to compare or contrast two elements in a sentence. It implies that the first element can be set aside or is less important compared to the second element. It often translates to 'putting A aside, B...'.

sentences.jp: デザインはともかく、機能はこの携帯電話が一番です。
sentences.en: As for design, the functionality of this phone is the best.

sentences.jp: 彼の容姿はともかく、性格は素晴らしいです。
sentences.en: Putting aside his looks, his personality is wonderful.

sentences.jp: 料理の味はともかく、盛り付けは綺麗です。
sentences.en: Putting aside the taste of the food, the presentation is beautiful.

sentences.jp: 値段はともかく、このバッグの質は良いです。
sentences.en: Ignoring the price, this bag’s quality is good.

title: ～はまだしも (〜wa mada shimo)
formation: Noun1 + はまだしも + Noun2
shortExplanation: Expresses that while the first item is somewhat acceptable, the second item is not.
longExplanation: The ～はまだしも grammar point is used to show a contrast where the first item or situation might still be understandable or tolerable, whereas the second one is harder to accept. It’s often translated as 'X is one thing, but Y...'.

sentences.jp: 失敗するのはまだしも、やらないで後悔する方がつらい。
sentences.en: Failing is one thing, but regretting not even trying is worse.

sentences.jp: このピザは野菜が少ないのはまだしも、味も悪い。
sentences.en: It’s not just that this pizza has few vegetables, it also tastes bad.

sentences.jp: たまに遅刻するのはまだしも、連絡もせずに遅刻するのは許せない。
sentences.en: Being late occasionally might be forgivable, but being late without any contact is unacceptable.

sentences.jp: 難しい質問に答えられないのはまだしも、簡単な質問にも答えられないのは問題だ。
sentences.en: It’s one thing not to be able to answer difficult questions, but it’s a real problem if you can’t even answer simple ones.

title: ～はもとより (〜wa moto yori)
formation: Noun1 + はもとより, Noun2 + も
shortExplanation: Expressing that something is obvious, and there is also something else.
longExplanation: The ～はもとより grammar point is used to emphasize that something is obvious or a given, and on top of that, there’s another aspect to consider. It translates to 'not only…but also…' or 'of course…and also…'.

sentences.jp: 英語はもとより、スペイン語も勉強しています。
sentences.en: I'm studying not only English, but also Spanish.

sentences.jp: 彼女は料理はもとより、お菓子作りも得意です。
sentences.en: She’s not only good at cooking, but also at making sweets.

sentences.jp: このホテルは部屋の清潔さはもとより、サービスも素晴らしいです。
sentences.en: This hotel not only has clean rooms but also great service.

sentences.jp: 彼は音楽はもとより、絵も上手です。
sentences.en: He’s not only good at music, but also skilled at drawing.

id: N2-～は抜きにして (〜wa nuki ni shite)
title: ～は抜きにして (〜wa nuki ni shite)
formation: Noun + は抜きにして
shortExplanation: Leaving aside, excluding.
longExplanation: The ～は抜きにして grammar point is used to express the idea of 'leaving aside' or 'excluding' a certain aspect. It is often used to focus the discussion or judgment on something else by disregarding the omitted point.

sentences.jp: 値段は抜きにして、味は美味しい。
sentences.en: Leaving aside the price, the taste is delicious.

sentences.jp: 彼の性格は抜きにして、彼の実力は認めざるを得ない。
sentences.en: Putting aside his personality, I have to acknowledge his ability.

sentences.jp: デザインは抜きにして、この携帯電話の機能は優れています。
sentences.en: Leaving aside the design, this phone’s features are excellent.

sentences.jp: 運転の速さは抜きにして、彼女は親切で信頼できる運転手だ。
sentences.en: Leaving aside her driving speed, she's a kind and trustworthy driver.

id: N2-～べきではない (〜beki dewa nai)
title: ～べきではない (〜beki dewa nai)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + べき + ではない
shortExplanation: Express that an action should not be done; 'should not', 'ought not to'.
longExplanation: The ～べきではない grammar point is used to indicate that an action is not recommended or should not be done. It can be translated as 'should not' or 'ought not to' in English. The typical formation is “Verb-dictionary form + べきではない.”

sentences.jp: 遅れる理由がないなら、遅れるべきではありません。
sentences.en: If there's no reason to be late, you shouldn’t be late.

sentences.jp: 子供に悪口を言うべきではありません。
sentences.en: You shouldn’t say hurtful things to children.

sentences.jp: この薬は一度に飲みすぎるべきではない。
sentences.en: You should not take too much of this medicine at once.

sentences.jp: 知らない人と個人情報を共有するべきではない。
sentences.en: You shouldn't share personal information with strangers.

title: ～まい (〜mai)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + まい
shortExplanation: Express a negative conjecture or determination; 'probably not' or 'I will not'.
longExplanation: The ～まい grammar point is used to express a negative conjecture or a strong determination not to do something. In modern usage, it is generally used with the dictionary form of verbs (来る→来るまい, する→するまい, etc.). It can be translated as 'probably not' or 'I will not' in English.

sentences.jp: 彼は今晩来るまい。
sentences.en: He probably won't come tonight.

sentences.jp: こんなに遅れては、電車に間に合うまい。
sentences.en: Being this late, I probably won't make it for the train.

sentences.jp: あの人は絶対に忘れるまい。
sentences.en: That person will definitely not forget.

sentences.jp: 私は二度とその失敗を繰り返すまい。
sentences.en: I will not repeat that mistake again.

title: ～まで～て (〜made 〜te)
formation: Time/Noun + まで + Verb(て-form)
shortExplanation: Express doing something until a certain time or condition; 'until', 'to'.
longExplanation: The ～まで～て grammar point is used to show that an action continues until a certain time or condition. In your examples, a time expression (such as 9時) is followed by まで, then the action in the て-form. It can be translated as 'until' or 'up to the point when/where'.

sentences.jp: 9時まで働いて、その後休憩しました。
sentences.en: I worked until 9 o'clock, and then took a break.

sentences.jp: 夜まで勉強して、試験に合格しました。
sentences.en: I studied until night, and passed the exam.

sentences.jp: 最後のページまで読んで、本を閉じました。
sentences.en: I read until the last page, then closed the book.

sentences.jp: 電車が着くまで待って、友達と会いました。
sentences.en: I waited until the train arrived, and then met my friend.

title: ～ままに (〜mama ni)
formation: Verb-て + まま, い-Adjective + のまま, な-Adjective + なまま, Noun + のまま
shortExplanation: Act according to; leave as is; unchanged.
longExplanation: The ～ままに grammar point is used to express leaving something in its current state or doing something exactly as is. It can also imply doing something naturally or without altering the circumstances. Depending on the part of speech, different particles are used: Verb-て + まま, い-Adjective + のまま, な-Adjective + なまま, Noun + のまま.

sentences.jp: 子供が寝ているままに、静かに部屋を出ました。
sentences.en: I quietly left the room while the child was still sleeping.

sentences.jp: 窓が開いたままで、寒くなりました。
sentences.en: It got cold with the window left open.

sentences.jp: 彼は元気なままで老人ホームに入りました。
sentences.en: He entered the nursing home while still in good health.

sentences.jp: 昔の思い出のままに、この町に戻りました。
sentences.en: I returned to this town with my old memories intact.

title: ～も～ば～も～ (〜mo〜ba〜mo〜)
formation: Noun/Verb/Adjective + も + (Verb/Adjective in ば-form) + も + (Verb/Adjective)
shortExplanation: Lists multiple attributes or actions applying to the same subject: 'both…and…', 'not only…but also…'.
longExplanation: 「～も～ば～も～」 is used when describing multiple characteristics or actions. It often appears with the conditional form (ば) plus も, indicating that 'A is true, and also B is true.' For example, 'He can do X, and he can also do Y.' It's different from '～ば～ほど', which means 'the more… the more…'.

sentences.jp: 彼は勉強もできればスポーツもできる。
sentences.en: He is good at studying and also at sports.

sentences.jp: このレストランは値段も安ければ味もいい。
sentences.en: This restaurant is cheap, and the food is also good.

sentences.jp: 彼女はピアノも弾けばギターも弾く。
sentences.en: She plays both the piano and the guitar.

sentences.jp: 人生には楽しい時もあれば辛い時もある。
sentences.en: In life, there are both fun times and tough times.

title: ～もかまわず (〜mo kamawazu)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + もかまわず, Noun + もかまわず
shortExplanation: Express indifference or disregard; 'without hesitating', 'without worrying about'.
longExplanation: The ～もかまわず grammar point is used to express indifference or disregard toward possible consequences or external factors. It can be translated as 'without hesitating', 'without worrying about', or 'regardless of'. It attaches to verbs in their dictionary form or nouns.

sentences.jp: 彼は周りの人もかまわず、大声で話しました。
sentences.en: Without worrying about the people around him, he spoke loudly.

sentences.jp: 彼女は時間もかまわず、ゆっくりと本を読み続けました。
sentences.en: She kept reading the book leisurely, without worrying about the time.

sentences.jp: 彼は寒さもかまわず、外で運動しました。
sentences.en: He exercised outside without worrying about the cold.

sentences.jp: 彼女は危険もかまわず、火事の中に飛び込んで助けに行きました。
sentences.en: She jumped into the fire without fear of danger to help.

title: ～ものか (〜mono ka)
formation: Verb-casual + ものか, い-Adjective + ものか, な-Adjective + (な)ものか, Noun + (な)ものか
shortExplanation: Definitely not; no way; no chance.
longExplanation: The ～ものか grammar point is used to express a strong denial, refusal, or astonishment. It often appears with a rhetorical question tone in casual forms, such as Verb (dictionary/volitional/～たい) + ものか, Adjective + ものか, or Noun + ものか, meaning 'No way...', 'As if I'd ever...', etc.

sentences.jp: そんなこと、僕がやりたいものか。
sentences.en: No way do I want to do such a thing.

sentences.jp: こんな仕事、誰が引き受けようものか。
sentences.en: Who on earth would take on a job like this? (No one would!)

sentences.jp: 誰が彼に助けを求めようものか。
sentences.en: Who would ever ask him for help? (Certainly no one!)

sentences.jp: この寒い日に外で遊ぶなんて、無理なものか。
sentences.en: There's no way we can play outside on a day this cold.

title: ～ものがある (〜mono ga aru)
formation: Verb-casual + ものがある, い-Adjective + ものがある, な-Adjective + なものがある
shortExplanation: Express a strong impression or feeling; 'there's something about it that...'
longExplanation: 「～ものがある」 is used to convey that there is a certain intangible quality or strong feeling about something that the speaker cannot ignore. It is often used with verbs (plain form), い-adjectives, or な-adjectives + な, to show that one ‘feels strongly that...’ or ‘there’s something about it that…’.

sentences.jp: 彼のスピーチには心を打たれるものがある。
sentences.en: There's something moving about his speech.

sentences.jp: この映画には考えさせられるものがある。
sentences.en: There's something about this movie that really makes you think.

sentences.jp: 彼の言うことにも一理あるものがある。
sentences.en: There's a certain point in what he says.

sentences.jp: 毎日忙しすぎる生活には少し息苦しいものがある。
sentences.en: There's something a bit suffocating about being so busy every day.

title: ～ものだ (〜mono da)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + ものだ, な-Adjective + なものだ, (Also: Verb-た form + ものだ for recollections)
shortExplanation: Expresses something natural or expected; 'it is normal that...', 'should', 'ought to'.
longExplanation: 「～ものだ」 can indicate that something is regarded as a general truth, common sense, or natural behavior. It can also express strong sentiment or emotion. In the past tense (Verb-た form + ものだ), it often shows nostalgia or recollection ('I used to...').

sentences.jp: 若い時はたくさん遊ぶものだ。
sentences.en: It is natural to have a lot of fun when you are young.

sentences.jp: 約束をしたら、守るものだ。
sentences.en: If you make a promise, you should keep it.

sentences.jp: お客さんが来ると、お茶を出すものだ。
sentences.en: When guests arrive, it's customary to serve tea.

sentences.jp: 人生は困難に立ち向かうものだ。
sentences.en: In life, one ought to face hardships.

title: ～ものだから (〜mono dakara)
formation: Verb-plain + ものだから, い-Adjective + ものだから, な-Adjective + なものだから, Noun + なものだから
shortExplanation: Express the reason or cause for something; 'because', 'since', 'due to'.
longExplanation: 「～ものだから」 is used to explain a reason or cause from the speaker’s point of view. It can be translated as 'because', 'since', or 'due to'. It often carries the nuance of 'you see, that's why ...'.

sentences.jp: 試験が近いものだから、毎日勉強しています。
sentences.en: Because the exam is near, I'm studying every day.

sentences.jp: このレストランはとても人気があるものだから、予約しないと席がないかもしれません。
sentences.en: Because this restaurant is very popular, there might not be any seats if we don't reserve.

sentences.jp: 彼は若いものだから、まだたくさんの経験を積んでいない。
sentences.en: Because he is young, he hasn't accumulated much experience yet.

sentences.jp: 今日は寒いものだから、手袋を持って行きましょう。
sentences.en: Since it's cold today, let's bring gloves.

id: N2-～ものではない (〜mono dewa nai)
title: ～ものではない (〜mono dewa nai)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + ものではない
shortExplanation: Expresses a strong negative suggestion or prohibition: 'should not', 'must not'.
longExplanation: 「～ものではない」 is used to indicate that something should not or must not be done. It often appears in written or formal contexts, meaning 'it is not proper to...' or 'you shouldn’t...'.

sentences.jp: そんなことを言うものではありません。
sentences.en: You should not say such a thing.

sentences.jp: 公共の場で大声を出すものではない。
sentences.en: You should not raise your voice in public places.

sentences.jp: 彼の秘密を他の人に教えるものではありません。
sentences.en: You must not tell his secret to other people.

sentences.jp: 安全な場所では遊んでいいが、危険なところで遊ぶものではない。
sentences.en: It's okay to play in safe places, but you should not play somewhere dangerous.

title: ～ものなら (〜mono nara)
formation: Verb-(potential/plain) form + ものなら, etc.
shortExplanation: Expresses a conditional wish or unlikely possibility; 'if only', 'if it were possible'.
longExplanation: 「～ものなら」 is used when expressing a strong wish under a hypothetical or difficult condition. It often implies that fulfilling this condition is unlikely or very hard, but 'if only it could happen, then...'.

sentences.jp: 帰れるものなら、今すぐ国へ帰りたい。
sentences.en: If I could possibly go back, I'd return to my country right now.

sentences.jp: できるものなら、世界を一周してみたい。
sentences.en: If it were possible, I'd love to travel around the world.

sentences.jp: やり直せるものなら、子供の頃に戻りたい。
sentences.en: If I could redo my life, I'd go back to my childhood.

sentences.jp: 元気になれるものなら、どんな薬でも試したい。
sentences.en: If it could make me healthy, I'd try any medicine.

title: ～ものの、～ (〜mono no、～)
formation: Verb-casual + ものの, い-Adjective + ものの, な-Adjective + なものの, Noun + であるものの
shortExplanation: Indicates contrast or contradiction between two clauses; 'although', 'but'.
longExplanation: 「～ものの」 is used to connect two parts of a sentence where the first part is true, yet the second part shows a contrasting or unexpected outcome. It can be translated as 'although', 'even though', or 'but still'.

sentences.jp: 早起きしたものの、バスに間に合わなかった。
sentences.en: Even though I got up early, I didn't catch the bus in time.

sentences.jp: この部屋は広いものの、家具が少ない。
sentences.en: Although this room is spacious, there isn't much furniture.

sentences.jp: 彼女は美しいものの、性格が悪い。
sentences.en: She is beautiful, but her personality is bad.

sentences.jp: 彼は有名な歌手であるものの、コンサートにあまり人が来なかった。
sentences.en: Although he is a famous singer, not many people came to his concert.

title: ～も同然だ (〜mo douzen da)
formation: Noun + (も)同然だ, Verb-casual + (も)同然だ, etc.
shortExplanation: Indicates 'as good as', 'virtually', or 'practically' the same as something.
longExplanation: 「～も同然だ」 means something is practically the same as (X). It emphasizes that although it may not be literally identical, there is hardly any difference in reality.

sentences.jp: 彼とは長い付き合いだから、家族も同然だ。
sentences.en: I've known him for so long that he's practically family.

sentences.jp: 毎日遅刻するなんて、来ていないも同然だ。
sentences.en: Being late every single day is as good as not coming at all.

sentences.jp: 彼女と私は幼なじみで、姉妹も同然の仲です。
sentences.en: She and I are childhood friends, practically like sisters.

sentences.jp: こんなに少ない給料じゃ、ただ働きも同然だ。
sentences.en: With such a small salary, it's practically like working for free.

title: ～やら～やら (〜yara〜yara)
formation: Verb-casual + やら, い-Adjective + やら, な-Adjective + やら, Noun + やら
shortExplanation: Expresses various items or actions, 'things like..., and so on'.
longExplanation: 「～やら～やら」 is used to list multiple things or actions in a somewhat non-exhaustive way. It can be translated as 'things like A and B' or 'A, B, and so on.' The grammar is often Verb-casual + やら, Noun + やら, い-Adjective + やら, etc.

sentences.jp: 宿題やら掃除やら、やることがたくさんある。
sentences.en: I have lots of things to do, like homework and cleaning.

sentences.jp: 野菜やら果物やら、色々な食べ物を買いました。
sentences.en: I bought all sorts of foods, such as vegetables and fruits.

sentences.jp: 彼女は歌がうまいやら踊れるやら、本当に多才だ。
sentences.en: She’s really talented—she can sing well, dance, and so on.

sentences.jp: お店にはバッグやら靴やらアクセサリーやらが置いてある。
sentences.en: The store has all kinds of items like bags, shoes, accessories, etc.

title: ～ようがない (〜you ga nai)
formation: Verb-ます stem + ようがない
shortExplanation: Expresses that there is no way (method) to do something; it's impossible to do.
longExplanation: 「～ようがない」 is used with the ます-stem of a verb to indicate that there is no means or method to carry out that action. It conveys the sense that it's impossible to do something or that there's absolutely no way to achieve it.

sentences.jp: 彼の連絡先がわからないので、助けようがない。
sentences.en: I don't know his contact information, so there's no way I can help him.

sentences.jp: ここまで壊れてしまったら、もう直しようがない。
sentences.en: Now that it's broken this badly, there's no way to fix it.

sentences.jp: 証拠が全くないから、彼が犯人だと証明しようがない。
sentences.en: There’s no evidence at all, so there’s no way to prove he’s the culprit.

sentences.jp: 突然英語で話しかけられて、何と答えればいいか分からず答えようがなかった。
sentences.en: I was suddenly spoken to in English and didn’t know how to respond, so there was no way for me to reply.

id: N2-～よりほかない (〜yori hoka nai)
title: ～よりほかない (〜yori hoka nai)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + よりほかない
shortExplanation: Express that there is no other choice; 'have no choice but', 'cannot do anything but'.
longExplanation: The ～よりほかない grammar point is used to express that there is no other choice or alternative in a given situation. It can be translated as 'have no choice but to...', 'nothing but...', or 'cannot do anything but...'.

sentences.jp: 試験が近いので、勉強するよりほかない。
sentences.en: Since the exam is near, there is no choice but to study.

sentences.jp: 風邪がひどいので、早く寝るよりほかない。
sentences.en: My cold is severe, so I have no choice but to go to bed early.

sentences.jp: バスが来ないので、歩くよりほかない。
sentences.en: Since the bus isn't coming, I have no choice but to walk.

sentences.jp: お金がないので、安いものを買うよりほかない。
sentences.en: Since I have no money, I have no choice but to buy cheap things.

title: ～わけがない (〜wake ga nai)
formation: Verb-casual + わけがない, い-Adjective + わけがない, な-Adjective + なわけがない, Noun + のわけがない
shortExplanation: Expresses 'there’s no way' or 'it’s impossible', indicating a high degree of certainty.
longExplanation: The ～わけがない grammar point is used to express that something is highly unlikely or impossible. It can be translated as 'there's no way' or 'it's impossible'.

sentences.jp: 彼が怪我をしたから、試合に勝てるわけがない。
sentences.en: He got injured, so there's no way he can win the match.

sentences.jp: 嘘ばかり言っている人を、信じるわけがない。
sentences.en: There's no way I can believe someone who constantly lies.

sentences.jp: 彼女は忙しいから、このパーティーに来るわけがない。
sentences.en: She's busy, so there's no way she can come to this party.

sentences.jp: 昨日はずっと寝ていたから、その事件を知るわけがない。
sentences.en: I was asleep all day yesterday, so there's no way I could know about that incident.

title: ～わけだ (〜wake da)
formation: Verb-casual + わけだ, い-Adjective + わけだ, な-Adjective + なわけだ, Noun + のわけだ
shortExplanation: Express a logical conclusion or reason; 'it means', 'no wonder', 'the reason is'.
longExplanation: The ～わけだ grammar point is used to express a logical conclusion or reason. It can be translated as 'it means...', 'no wonder...', or 'that explains why...'.

sentences.jp: 彼女が遅れたのは、電車が遅れたわけだ。
sentences.en: The reason she was late is that the train was delayed.

sentences.jp: 授業が面白かったから、みんな熱心に聞いていたわけだ。
sentences.en: The class was interesting, so that's why everyone was listening intently.

sentences.jp: 彼はとても忙しいですから、休みがほとんどないわけだ。
sentences.en: He's really busy, which is why he hardly has any time off.

sentences.jp: この問題が難しいから、全然わからないわけだ。
sentences.en: This problem is tough, so no wonder I don't understand it at all.

id: N2-～わけではない (〜wake dewa nai)
title: ～わけではない (〜wake dewa nai)
formation: Verb-plain + わけではない, い-Adjective + わけではない, な-Adjective + だわけではない, Noun + だわけではない
shortExplanation: Not necessarily; it doesn't mean that ~.
longExplanation: The ～わけではない grammar point is used to express that something is not necessarily the case or 'it doesn’t mean that...' It negates a broad assumption or generalization.

sentences.jp: 彼が勉強しないわけではないが、成績があまり良くない。
sentences.en: It's not that he doesn't study, but his grades aren't very good.

sentences.jp: このレストランが高いわけではないけど、毎日通うには少し高いね。
sentences.en: It's not that this restaurant is expensive, but it's a bit too pricey to go there every day.

sentences.jp: 彼女が優しくないわけではないが、時々話し方がきついことがある。
sentences.en: It's not that she's unkind, but sometimes her manner of speaking can be harsh.

sentences.jp: 彼が忙しいわけではないが、時間を上手に使えていないようだ。
sentences.en: It's not that he's busy; he just doesn't seem to use his time effectively.

id: N2-～わけにはいかない (〜wake ni wa ikanai)
title: ～わけにはいかない (〜wake ni wa ikanai)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + わけにはいかない
shortExplanation: Expresses that one cannot (or must not) do something due to responsibility, social pressure, etc.
longExplanation: The ～わけにはいかない grammar point is used to express that you cannot afford to do something or are not allowed to do it. It often implies a sense of responsibility, moral/social obligation, or external pressure that prevents you from doing it.

sentences.jp: 試験が近いから、遊ぶわけにはいかない。
sentences.en: Since the exam is close, I can't afford to just play around.

sentences.jp: 田中さんが待っているので、遅れるわけにはいかない。
sentences.en: Mr. Tanaka is waiting, so I absolutely must not be late.

sentences.jp: 子供たちのために、この仕事を辞めるわけにはいかない。
sentences.en: For the sake of my kids, I can't afford to quit this job.

sentences.jp: この問題は重要だから、無視するわけにはいかない。
sentences.en: This issue is important, so I can't simply ignore it.

title: ～を～として (〜wo〜toshite)
formation: Noun1 + を + Noun2 + として
shortExplanation: Express 'as', 'in the capacity of', or 'taking something as...'.
longExplanation: The grammar pattern 「～を～として」 is used with nouns to indicate taking one thing 'as' or 'in the role of' another. It shows how a certain noun is being viewed or utilized.

sentences.jp: 彼は教師を職業として働いています。
sentences.en: He works as a teacher.

sentences.jp: 彼女はリーダーを役割として受け入れました。
sentences.en: She accepted the role of leader.

sentences.jp: 私たちは彼を仲間として信頼しています。
sentences.en: We trust him as one of our own (a close companion).

sentences.jp: このアーティストは伝統を基盤として作品を作ります。
sentences.en: This artist creates works with tradition as the foundation.

id: N2-～をきっかけに (〜wo kikkake ni)
title: ～をきっかけに (〜wo kikkake ni)
formation: Noun + をきっかけに
shortExplanation: Indicates that something serves as a trigger or starting point for another action/event.
longExplanation: 「～をきっかけに」 is used with a noun to show that an event, thing, or situation became a trigger or motive that led to a new development or action. It translates as 'taking ... as an opportunity,' 'due to ...,' or 'inspired by ...'.

sentences.jp: 結婚をきっかけに、彼は真剣に仕事を頑張り始めた。
sentences.en: Getting married was the catalyst for him to start working seriously.

sentences.jp: あの出会いをきっかけに、私たちは友達になりました。
sentences.en: That encounter led us to become friends.

sentences.jp: コンサートをきっかけに、彼女は音楽の道に進むことを決めました。
sentences.en: Inspired by the concert, she decided to pursue music.

sentences.jp: 留学をきっかけに、私の外国語スキルが向上しました。
sentences.en: Studying abroad gave me the chance to improve my foreign language skills.

title: ～を中心に (〜wo chuushin ni)
formation: Noun + を中心に
shortExplanation: Indicates that something is centered around or focused on a main topic or area.
longExplanation: 「～を中心に」 is used with nouns to show that an action or situation is mainly or primarily about the specified noun. It can be translated as 'centered on', 'focusing on', or 'with ... as the main focus'.

sentences.jp: このイベントは音楽を中心に楽しまれています。
sentences.en: This event is mainly enjoyed for its music.

sentences.jp: 彼の仕事は東京を中心に行われています。
sentences.en: His work is primarily conducted in and around Tokyo.

sentences.jp: 彼女は健康を中心に本を書いています。
sentences.en: She’s writing a book focusing on health.

sentences.jp: 最近の研究は環境保護を中心に行われている。
sentences.en: Recent research is being carried out with a primary focus on environmental protection.

title: ～を問わず (〜wo towazu)
formation: Noun + を問わず
shortExplanation: Means 'regardless of' or 'irrespective of'.
longExplanation: 「～を問わず」 is used to express that something applies or is available without any distinction regarding the stated nouns/conditions. It means 'regardless of...' or 'irrespective of...'.

sentences.jp: このイベントは、年齢を問わず、誰でも参加できます。
sentences.en: This event is open to everyone, regardless of age.

sentences.jp: このスーパーは、日曜日を問わず、毎日開いています。
sentences.en: This supermarket is open every day, regardless of Sunday.

sentences.jp: 彼女は、暑さ寒さを問わず、毎朝散歩をしています。
sentences.en: She takes a walk every morning, no matter the heat or cold.

sentences.jp: 社長は、国籍を問わず、すべての社員を平等に扱っています。
sentences.en: The company president treats all employees equally, regardless of nationality.

title: ～を込めて (〜wo komete)
formation: Feeling/emotion/intention Noun + を + 込めて + Verb
shortExplanation: To express that something is done with a certain feeling, intention, or emotion.
longExplanation: The ～を込めて grammar point is used to express that an action is done with a certain feeling, intention or emotion. It can be translated as 'with' or 'filled with' in English. ～を込めて is typically used with feelings or emotions that the speaker wishes to convey through their actions.

sentences.jp: 愛を込めて料理を作りました。
sentences.en: I cooked the meal with love.

sentences.jp: 感謝を込めて先生にお礼の手紙を書きました。
sentences.en: I wrote a thank-you letter to my teacher, filled with gratitude.

sentences.jp: 彼は全力を込めて最後の瞬間まで戦いました。
sentences.en: He fought until the last moment with all his strength.

sentences.jp: 彼女は悲しみを込めて友達へ別れの言葉を伝えました。
sentences.en: She conveyed her farewell words to her friend with sadness.

title: ～を通じて (〜wo tsuujite)
formation: Noun + を通じて
shortExplanation: Expresses 'through', 'throughout', or 'during'.
longExplanation: The ～を通じて grammar point is used to express actions or conditions that occur through or throughout a specific period, medium, or method. It can be translated as 'through', 'throughout', or 'during' in English. It is formed by attaching 'を通じて' to a noun that represents time, place, or method.

sentences.jp: インターネットを通じて友達と連絡を取ります。
sentences.en: I keep in touch with my friends through the internet.

sentences.jp: 彼は一日を通じて熱心に働いています。
sentences.en: He works diligently throughout the day.

sentences.jp: このイベントは週末を通じて開催されます。
sentences.en: This event will take place throughout the weekend.

sentences.jp: その花は春を通じて咲いています。
sentences.en: The flowers bloom throughout the spring.

title: ～を頼りに (〜wo tayori ni)
formation: Noun + を頼りに
shortExplanation: To rely on or depend on something or someone.
longExplanation: The ～を頼りに grammar point is used to express that someone is relying on or depending on something or someone. It can be translated as 'to rely on', 'to depend on', or 'counting on' in English. This phrase is used with nouns (people or objects) that act as a source of support or help.

sentences.jp: 彼女は地図を頼りに旅行をしている。
sentences.en: She is traveling relying on a map.

sentences.jp: 多くの人が彼の力を頼りに生活している。
sentences.en: Many people are living depending on his strength.

sentences.jp: このプロジェクトでは、君のスキルを頼りにしています。
sentences.en: In this project, we are counting on your skills.

sentences.jp: 彼は友達の助けを頼りに問題を解決しようとした。
sentences.en: He tried to solve the problem relying on his friend's help.

title: ～一方 (〜ippou)
formation: Verb-casual + 一方(で), い-Adjective + 一方(で), な-Adjective + な/である一方(で), Noun + である一方(で)
shortExplanation: Indicates that while one action or situation is happening, another action or situation is also occurring or is in contrast.
longExplanation: The ～一方 grammar point is used to express that while one action or situation is taking place, another is occurring at the same time or in contrast. The clauses connected by 一方 often show simultaneous actions, opposing characteristics, or parallel developments.

sentences.jp: 技術が進化する一方、生活が忙しくなっている。
sentences.en: While technology advances, life is getting busier.

sentences.jp: 日本語が上達する一方、英語力が下がっている気がする。
sentences.en: While my Japanese improves, I feel like my English ability is declining.

sentences.jp: この料理は美味しい一方、ちょっと高いです。
sentences.en: While this dish is delicious, it’s a bit expensive.

sentences.jp: 彼は優しい一方、厳しい面もある。
sentences.en: While he is kind, he also has a strict side.

title: ～一方だ (〜ippou da)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + 一方だ
shortExplanation: Expresses that a situation or condition is continuing to increase or decrease in one direction.
longExplanation: The ～一方だ grammar point is used when something is trending in a single direction over time, often increasing or worsening. It emphasizes that the tendency keeps going in one direction.

sentences.jp: 世界の人口は増える一方だ。
sentences.en: The world's population continues to increase.

sentences.jp: この町の治安は悪化する一方だ。
sentences.en: Public safety in this town is getting worse and worse.

sentences.jp: 僕の日本語は上達する一方だ。
sentences.en: My Japanese keeps improving.

sentences.jp: 夏になると、暑さが厳しくなる一方だ。
sentences.en: As summer arrives, it just keeps getting hotter.

title: ～上で (〜ue de)
formation: Verb-ますstem + 上で, Noun + の上で
shortExplanation: Used to express that something is done after or on the basis of something else; 'after', 'upon'.
longExplanation: The ～上で grammar point is used to indicate that an action or decision is made after another action or condition, or based on certain information. It can be translated as 'after doing something' or 'on the basis of...'.

sentences.jp: 調査を行った上で、結論を出しましょう。
sentences.en: Let's draw a conclusion after conducting the investigation.

sentences.jp: 就職活動を終えた上で、進学するかどうか決めます。
sentences.en: I will decide whether to continue my studies after finishing my job hunting.

sentences.jp: 試着した上で、この服を買うかどうか考えます。
sentences.en: I'll think about whether to buy these clothes after trying them on.

sentences.jp: 経験者の意見を聞いた上で、プロジェクトの方向性を決定しましょう。
sentences.en: Let's decide the direction of the project after listening to those with experience.

title: ～上に (〜ue ni)
formation: Verb-plain + 上に, い-Adjective + 上に, な-Adjective + な上に, Noun + の上に
shortExplanation: Express 'in addition to', 'besides', 'not only... but also'.
longExplanation: The ～上に grammar point is used to indicate that there is an additional piece of information or situation besides the first one. It can be translated as 'in addition to', 'besides', or 'not only... but also'. It can be used with verbs, い-adjectives, な-adjectives, and nouns.

sentences.jp: 彼は頭がいい上に、スポーツも得意です。
sentences.en: In addition to being smart, he is also good at sports.

sentences.jp: このドレスは美しい上に、値段も安いです。
sentences.en: This dress is not only beautiful but also affordable.

sentences.jp: 彼女は優しい上に、料理も上手です。
sentences.en: Besides being kind, she is also good at cooking.

sentences.jp: 彼は社長の上に、有名な作家でもある。
sentences.en: Not only is he a company president, but he is also a famous writer.

title: ～上は (～ue wa)
formation: Verb-casual + 上は, い-Adjective + 上は, な-Adjective + な上は, Noun + の上は
shortExplanation: Expresses 'now that', 'since', or 'once'.
longExplanation: The ～上は grammar point is used to express that since a certain condition or situation exists, a certain action or result follows. It can be translated as 'now that', 'since', or 'once' in English. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

sentences.jp: 試験に合格した上は、パーティーを開こう。
sentences.en: Now that I've passed the exam, let's have a party.

sentences.jp: この仕事を引き受けた上は、最後までやり遂げるつもりだ。
sentences.en: Now that I have taken on this job, I intend to see it through to the end.

sentences.jp: 親切だと言われた上は、手伝ってあげよう。
sentences.en: Since you said I'm kind, I'll help you out.

sentences.jp: 甘いものが好きな上は、このデザートをきっと気に入るでしょう。
sentences.en: Now that you like sweets, you'll surely enjoy this dessert.

title: ～以上 (〜ijou)
formation: Verb-casual + 以上, い-Adjective + 以上, な-Adjective + な以上, Noun + の以上
shortExplanation: Expresses 'since' or 'as long as' in a conditional context.
longExplanation: The ～以上 grammar point is used to express a condition or requirement, and it can be translated as 'since', 'as long as', or 'if'. It often emphasizes that if a certain condition is met, a certain action or consequence will follow.

sentences.jp: 彼は忙しい以上、手伝いに行っても無駄だろう。
sentences.en: Since he's busy, it would be pointless to try helping him.

sentences.jp: 約束した以上、守るべきだ。
sentences.en: Since I made a promise, I should keep it.

sentences.jp: 締め切りが近い以上、全力で仕事をしなければならない。
sentences.en: As long as the deadline is near, I have to work with all my might.

sentences.jp: 彼女は大学生である以上、勉強が優先されるべきだ。
sentences.en: Since she's a college student, studying should be her priority.

title: ～以来 (〜irai)
formation: Verb-past + 以来, い-Adjective + 以来, Noun + 以来
shortExplanation: Indicates something happening 'since', 'ever since', or 'after' a particular time.
longExplanation: The ～以来 grammar point is used to express a period of time that has continued uninterrupted since a certain point in the past. It can be translated as 'since', 'ever since', or 'after'. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, an adjective, or a noun.

sentences.jp: 卒業以来、彼女に会っていない。
sentences.en: I haven't seen her since we graduated.

sentences.jp: 子供が生まれて以来、忙しくなった。
sentences.en: I've been busy ever since my child was born.

sentences.jp: 東京に引っ越して以来、友達が増えた。
sentences.en: I've made more friends since I moved to Tokyo.

sentences.jp: あの映画を見た以来、彼女は怖がりになった。
sentences.en: She's been easily frightened ever since she watched that movie.

title: ～切る (〜kiru)
formation: Verb-stem + 切る
shortExplanation: To do something completely or thoroughly.
longExplanation: The ～切る grammar point is added to the verb stem (masu-form) to express doing something completely or thoroughly, often implying a strong sense of finishing or using up everything.

sentences.jp: 彼は試験勉強をがんばり切るつもりだ。
sentences.en: He intends to give his absolute all to exam study.

sentences.jp: この仕事を終わり切るまで、帰らないでください。
sentences.en: Please don't go home until you've completely finished this job.

sentences.jp: 友達と喧嘩して、何も言い切れなかった。
sentences.en: I argued with my friend and couldn't say everything I wanted to say.

sentences.jp: 彼らは、そのゲームをやり切ることができました。
sentences.en: They were able to complete that game thoroughly.

title: ～反面 (〜hanmen)
formation: Verb-casual + 反面, い-Adjective + 反面, な-Adjective + である反面, Noun + である反面
shortExplanation: Express contrast between two aspects; 'on the one hand', 'on the other hand'.
longExplanation: The ～反面 grammar point is used to show a contrast between two aspects of something. It can be translated as 'on the one hand' or 'on the other hand'.

sentences.jp: この車は速い反面、燃費が悪いです。
sentences.en: On the one hand, this car is fast, but on the other hand, it has poor fuel efficiency.

sentences.jp: 彼は頭がいい反面、運動が苦手です。
sentences.en: On the one hand, he is smart, but on the other hand, he's not good at sports.

sentences.jp: 彼女は親切である反面、面倒見が良すぎることがある。
sentences.en: On the one hand, she is kind, but on the other hand, she can be too helpful.

sentences.jp: この仕事は給料が高い反面、ストレスがたくさんあります。
sentences.en: On the one hand, this job pays well, but on the other hand, it's quite stressful.

title: ～向け (〜muke)
formation: Noun + 向け
shortExplanation: Indicating target or intended audience.
longExplanation: The ～向け grammar point is used to indicate the target or intended audience for a product, service, or event. It can be translated as 'for', 'aimed at', or 'intended for'.

sentences.jp: この雑誌は若者向けです。
sentences.en: This magazine is aimed at young people.

sentences.jp: スーパーで家族向けの食品セットが売っています。
sentences.en: The supermarket sells food sets intended for families.

sentences.jp: このアプリは初心者向けに作られています。
sentences.en: This application is designed for beginners.

sentences.jp: このレストランはビーガン向けのメニューが充実しています。
sentences.en: This restaurant has an extensive menu aimed at vegans.

title: ～恐れがある (〜osore ga aru)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + 恐れがある
shortExplanation: Express a concern or fear that something undesirable might occur.
longExplanation: The ～恐れがある grammar point indicates that there is a concern or fear that something undesirable or negative might happen. It is often used for potential problems or risks, and can be translated as 'there is a risk' or 'there is a possibility'.

sentences.jp: 地震が来る恐れがあるため、避難所に行ってください。
sentences.en: Please go to the evacuation center because there is a risk of an earthquake.

sentences.jp: 彼は事故に遭う恐れがあるので、注意して運転してください。
sentences.en: There's a possibility he might have an accident, so please drive carefully.

sentences.jp: この薬を飲みすぎると、副作用の恐れがある。
sentences.en: If you take too much of this medicine, there is a risk of side effects.

sentences.jp: 低い予算でこのプロジェクトを進めると、品質が低くなる恐れがある。
sentences.en: If we proceed with this project on a low budget, the quality may suffer.

title: ～折には (〜ori ni wa)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + 折には, Verb-た form + 折には, い-Adjective + 折には, な-Adjective + な折には, Noun + の折には
shortExplanation: Expresses 'when' or 'in case of' specific situations or timings.
longExplanation: The ～折には grammar point is used to emphasize a specific moment or situation (often not very frequent). It can be translated as 'when (the occasion arises)' or 'in case of'.

sentences.jp: 喉が渇く折には、このペットボトルの水を飲んでください。
sentences.en: When you get thirsty, please drink water from this bottle.

sentences.jp: この地域に来る折には、この神社に必ずお参りしましょう。
sentences.en: When you visit this area, make sure to go to this shrine.

sentences.jp: 彼が元気じゃない折には、具合が悪いか尋ねてください。
sentences.en: If he doesn't seem well, please ask if he's feeling sick.

sentences.jp: チャンスがある折には、躊躇せずに行動しましょう。
sentences.en: When you have an opportunity, act without hesitation.

title: ～末 (～sue)
formation: Noun + 末
shortExplanation: Express the outcome after a period of time or series of actions; 'after', 'as a result of'.
longExplanation: The ～末 grammar point is used to indicate an outcome or result after a period of time, effort, or a series of actions. It can be translated as 'after', 'as a result of', or 'following'. It is typically used with nouns (e.g., '相談の末', '努力の末').

sentences.jp: 長い議論の末、ようやく意見がまとまった。
sentences.en: After a long discussion, we finally reached a consensus.

sentences.jp: 一週間の勉強の末、試験に合格した。
sentences.en: After a week of studying, I passed the exam.

sentences.jp: 何度も失敗の末、成功を手に入れた。
sentences.en: After many failures, I finally achieved success.

sentences.jp: 彼との別れの末、新しい人生を始めた。
sentences.en: After breaking up with him, I started a new life.

title: ～次第 (〜shidai)
formation: Verb-ますstem + 次第, Noun + 次第
shortExplanation: Expresses 'as soon as' (when used with Verb-ますstem) or 'depending on' (when used with a noun).
longExplanation: The grammar pattern ～次第 can mean 'as soon as' when used with a verb in its ます-stem, and 'depending on' when used with a noun. For instance, 仕事が終わり次第 (as soon as work finishes) or 天気次第 (depending on the weather).

sentences.jp: 仕事が終わり次第、帰ります。
sentences.en: I will go home as soon as I finish work.

sentences.jp: お客様のご意見次第で、サービスを改善します。
sentences.en: We will improve our services depending on customer feedback.

sentences.jp: 彼の返事次第で、パーティーに行くかどうか決めます。
sentences.en: I will decide whether or not to go to the party depending on his answer.

sentences.jp: 天気次第で、ピクニックを予定しています。
sentences.en: We are planning a picnic depending on the weather.

title: ～次第で (〜shidai de)
formation: Noun + 次第で
shortExplanation: Indicates 'depending on; based on' a certain factor or situation.
longExplanation: The ～次第で pattern typically follows a noun to show that something is decided or changes 'depending on' that factor. It can be translated as 'based on...' or 'depending on...'.

sentences.jp: 仕事の終わり次第で、家に帰る時間が変わります。
sentences.en: Depending on when I finish work, the time I go home may change.

sentences.jp: 天気次第で、ピクニックに行く予定です。
sentences.en: We plan to go on a picnic depending on the weather.

sentences.jp: 彼の気分次第で、パーティーに参加するかどうか決まります。
sentences.en: Whether he participates in the party depends on his mood.

sentences.jp: 会議の進行次第で、決定が変わるかもしれません。
sentences.en: The decision may change depending on how the meeting progresses.

title: ～次第です (〜shidai desu)
formation: Verb-ますstem + 次第です (or Noun + 次第です)
shortExplanation: A polite/formal way to say 'it depends on...' or 'as soon as...', often used in concluding statements.
longExplanation: 「～次第です」 is frequently used in more formal contexts, either to mean that something depends on certain conditions or to indicate an action will happen as soon as something else is completed. It’s also used as a set phrase to conclude an explanation in writing.

sentences.jp: 天気次第ですが、明日はピクニックに行こうと思います。
sentences.en: It depends on the weather, but I’m thinking of going for a picnic tomorrow.

sentences.jp: 試験の結果次第ですが、留学したいと考えています。
sentences.en: It depends on my exam results, but I'm considering studying abroad.

sentences.jp: お客様のご意見次第で新メニューを追加する次第です。
sentences.en: We intend to add a new menu item depending on our customers’ feedback.

sentences.jp: 申請が完了次第ですので、手続きが終わったらパスポートを受け取れます。
sentences.en: As soon as the application is completed, you'll be able to receive your passport.

title: ～気味 (〜gimi)
formation: Noun + 気味 (e.g., 風邪気味, 寝不足気味, 緊張気味)
shortExplanation: Indicates a slight feeling or tendency toward a particular state.
longExplanation: The suffix ～気味 generally attaches to nouns (or nominalized words) to express a slight feeling, tendency, or inclination toward a particular state. It often translates to '-ish' or 'a bit' in English (e.g., 'sleep-deprived-ish', 'nervous-ish').

sentences.jp: 最近寝不足気味で、ちょっと疲れています。
sentences.en: Lately, I'm feeling somewhat sleep-deprived and a bit tired.

sentences.jp: この部屋は暗い気味だから、もっと明るい照明をつけよう。
sentences.en: This room is a bit dark, so let's install brighter lighting.

sentences.jp: 彼女は緊張気味で話していました。
sentences.en: She was speaking somewhat nervously.

sentences.jp: 彼の声は風邪気味だったね。
sentences.en: His voice sounded like he might have a slight cold.

title: ～限り (〜kagiri)
formation: Verb-casual + 限り, い-Adjective + 限り, な-Adjective + な限り, Noun + の限り
shortExplanation: Expresses 'as long as', 'while', or 'to the extent that' a certain condition holds true.
longExplanation: The phrase ～限り is used to indicate that something continues or is true 'as long as' or 'while' a certain condition is met. It can also mean 'to the extent that'.

sentences.jp: 元気な限り、運動し続けます。
sentences.en: As long as I'm healthy, I'll keep exercising.

sentences.jp: お金がある限り、旅行を楽しみたい。
sentences.en: As long as I have money, I'd like to travel.

sentences.jp: 彼が話し続ける限り、このプロジェクトのメリットが伝わるでしょう。
sentences.en: As long as he keeps talking about it, the benefits of this project will be conveyed.

sentences.jp: 勉強する限り、試験に合格できる可能性が高まります。
sentences.en: As long as you study, you'll have a higher chance of passing the exam.

title: ～際に (〜sai ni)
formation: Verb-casual + 際に, い-Adjective + 際に, な-Adjective + な際に, Noun + の際に
shortExplanation: Means 'when', 'while', or 'on the occasion of' a certain event or situation.
longExplanation: ～際に is used to specify a time or occasion when something happens. It’s more formal than a simple 'とき' and often appears in announcements or polite instructions.

sentences.jp: 出発の際に、パスポートを忘れないでください。
sentences.en: When departing, please don't forget your passport.

sentences.jp: 地震が起こる際に、すぐに避難してください。
sentences.en: When an earthquake occurs, please evacuate immediately.

sentences.jp: 日本へ来る際に、お土産をたくさん買うつもりです。
sentences.en: When I come to Japan, I plan to buy lots of souvenirs.

sentences.jp: 面接の際に、スーツを着用してください。
sentences.en: Please wear a suit when attending the interview.

id: N2-A あるいは B (A aruiwa B)
title: A あるいは B (A aruiwa B)
formation: Phrase A + あるいは + Phrase B
shortExplanation: Indicates alternatives; 'A or B', 'either A or B'.
longExplanation: The あるいは grammar point is used to show alternatives or options, similar to 'A or B' or 'either A or B' in English. It highlights the possibility of more than one option in a situation. It can be used with verbs, adjectives, and nouns.

sentences.jp: 今日は映画を見るあるいはショッピングをする予定です。
sentences.en: Today, I plan to either watch a movie or go shopping.

sentences.jp: 彼女は英語あるいはドイツ語で話すことができます。
sentences.en: She can speak either English or German.

sentences.jp: 僕らはコーヒーあるいは紅茶を飲むのが好きです。
sentences.en: We like to drink either coffee or tea.

sentences.jp: この仕事は正社員あるいは契約社員として働くことができます。
sentences.en: You can work as either a full-time employee or a contract employee in this job.

id: N2-A すなわち B。 (A sunawachi B)
title: A すなわち B。 (A sunawachi B)
formation: Phrase A + すなわち + Phrase B
shortExplanation: Express that A is equivalent to B; 'in other words', 'namely', 'that is to say'.
longExplanation: The grammar point A すなわち B is used to express that two phrases are equivalent or that the second phrase is further explanation, clarification or the true meaning of the first phrase. This can be translated as 'in other words', 'namely', or 'that is to say' in English.

sentences.jp: 私は立派な成果を出したい、すなわち、成功したいのです。
sentences.en: I want to achieve great results, in other words, I want to succeed.

sentences.jp: 彼は言葉に詰まった、すなわち、何も言えなかった。
sentences.en: He was at a loss for words, that is to say, he couldn't say anything.

sentences.jp: 彼女は自分の目標に向かって努力し続けている、すなわち、彼女は非常に頑張っている。
sentences.en: She keeps working hard towards her goals, namely, she is trying very hard.

sentences.jp: この店はお客様の満足を一番大事にしています、すなわち、サービスが良いということです。
sentences.en: This store values customer satisfaction above all else, in other words, it has good service.

id: N2-A それはそうと B。 (A Sore wa sou to B)
title: A それはそうと B。 (A Sore wa sou to B)
formation: Sentence A + それはそうと + Sentence B
shortExplanation: Changing the topic; 'by the way', 'speaking of which'.
longExplanation: The grammar point A それはそうと B is used to change the topic or bring up a new subject in a conversation. It can be translated as 'by the way' or 'speaking of which' in English. It's used to connect two unrelated sentences or ideas, with A being the original topic and B being the new topic brought up.

sentences.jp: それはそうと、今日の天気はいいですね。
sentences.en: By the way, the weather is nice today, isn't it?

sentences.jp: 映画は面白かったです。それはそうと、この店のラーメンはおいしいですよ。
sentences.en: The movie was interesting. Speaking of which, this restaurant's ramen is delicious.

sentences.jp: 明日から夏休みですね。それはそうと、新しいカフェがオープンしたみたいです。
sentences.en: Summer vacation starts tomorrow, huh? By the way, it seems a new cafe has opened.

sentences.jp: 昨日の試験は難しかった。それはそうと、週末にパーティーがあるのを知ってますか？
sentences.en: Yesterday's exam was difficult. Speaking of which, do you know there's a party this weekend?

title: A。おまけに B。(~omake ni)
formation: Sentence A + おまけに  + Sentence B
shortExplanation: Expresses that in addition to A, there is also B; 'moreover', 'besides', 'not only', 'but also'.
longExplanation: The A。おまけに B。grammar point is used to express that in addition to the first statement A, there is also the second statement B which often emphasizes the negative aspect or difficulty of the situation. It can be translated as 'moreover', 'besides', 'not only', or 'but also' in English. The formation is used with two sentences, placing おまけに between those two sentences.

sentences.jp: 彼は遅刻した。おまけに宿題も忘れた。
sentences.en: He was late. Moreover, he forgot his homework.

sentences.jp: このアパートは狭い。おまけに家賃が高い。
sentences.en: This apartment is small. Besides, the rent is high.

sentences.jp: 彼女は仕事が忙しい。おまけに子供も小さい。
sentences.en: She is busy with work. Not only that, her children are also small.

sentences.jp: 昨日は雨が降った。おまけに風も強かった。
sentences.en: Yesterday it rained. In addition, the wind was strong.

title: A。さて B。(A. Sate B.)
formation: A + さて + B
shortExplanation: Indicates a sudden change or shift in the topic or situation; 'now then', 'well', 'so'.
longExplanation: The さて grammar point is used to indicate a sudden change or shift in the topic or situation. It often expresses a sense of moving on to the next topic or action, acting as a connector between sentences. It can be loosely translated as 'now then', 'well', or 'so' in English.

sentences.jp: 今日の仕事が終わった。さて、これから帰るか。
sentences.en: Today's work is done. Now then, shall we go home?

sentences.jp: 話がちょっと長くなったね。さて、もっと喜美子さんのことを教えてください。
sentences.en: The conversation got a bit lengthy. Well, please tell me more about Kimiko.

sentences.jp: 映画を見て楽しかった。さて、何を食べに行こう。
sentences.en: Watching the movie was fun. So, what shall we go eat?

sentences.jp: マラソンが終わった！さて、少し休んでからシャワーを浴びよう。
sentences.en: The marathon is over! Now then, let's rest a bit and then take a shower.

title: A。しかも B。(A. Shikamo B.)
formation: Statement A + 。しかも + Statement B。
shortExplanation: Expressing additional information; 'moreover', 'besides', 'furthermore'.
longExplanation: The しかも grammar point is used to express additional information, supplementing or emphasizing what was mentioned before. It can be translated as 'moreover', 'besides', or 'furthermore' in English. Both A and B are independent statements, and しかも is used to connect them.

sentences.jp: この料理は美味しいです。しかも、安いです。
sentences.en: This dish is delicious. Moreover, it is cheap.

sentences.jp: 彼は優秀な学生です。しかも、スポーツが得意です。
sentences.en: He is an excellent student. Besides, he is good at sports.

sentences.jp: 彼女は歌が上手です。しかも、ダンスも上手です。
sentences.en: She is good at singing. Furthermore, she is also good at dancing.

sentences.jp: このカフェは雰囲気がいいです。しかも、コーヒーも美味しいです。
sentences.en: This cafe has a nice atmosphere. Moreover, the coffee is also delicious.

id: N2-A。したがって B。(A. Shitagatte B.)
title: A。したがって B。(A. Shitagatte B.)
formation: A + したがって + B
shortExplanation: Express a cause-and-effect relationship; 'therefore', 'consequently', 'so'.
longExplanation: The したがって grammar point is used to express a cause-and-effect relationship between two sentences, where 'A' is the cause and 'B' is the effect, result or conclusion. It can be translated as 'therefore', 'consequently', or 'so' in English.

sentences.jp: 今日は雨が降っています。したがって、傘を持って行きましょう。
sentences.en: It is raining today. Therefore, let's bring an umbrella.

sentences.jp: 彼は忙しいです。したがって、彼に連絡するのはやめましょう。
sentences.en: He is busy. Consequently, let's not contact him.

sentences.jp: そのレストランは人気があります。したがって、予約しないと席がないかもしれません。
sentences.en: That restaurant is popular. Therefore, there might be no seats if you don't make a reservation.

sentences.jp: 試験が近いです。したがって、勉強に集中しましょう。
sentences.en: The exam is coming up soon. Consequently, let's focus on studying.

title: A。すると B。(~suruto)
formation: A (Sentence) + すると + B (Sentence)
shortExplanation: Indicates that when action A is done, action or event B follows; 'then', 'when', 'if'.
longExplanation: The A。すると B。grammar point is used to express that when action A is done or takes place, action or event B follows. It can be translated as 'then', 'when', or 'if' in English. It is used to show a natural consequence or result of action A.

sentences.jp: 電話をかけると、彼女が出ました。
sentences.en: When I made the phone call, she answered.

sentences.jp: このボタンを押すと、ドアが開きます。
sentences.en: If you press this button, the door will open.

sentences.jp: コンビニに行くと、たまたま友達に会いました。
sentences.en: When I went to the convenience store, I happened to meet my friend.

sentences.jp: 彼が家を出ると、すぐに雨が降り始めました。
sentences.en: As soon as he left the house, it started to rain.

title: A。そういえば B。(~souieba)
formation: A + そういえば + B
shortExplanation: Recalling something related to A; 'speaking of which', 'by the way', 'now that you mention it'.
longExplanation: The A。そういえば B。grammar point is used when something related to A is recalled or brought up. It can be translated as 'speaking of which', 'by the way', or 'now that you mention it' in English. It is commonly used to connect two related topics or ideas in a conversation and indicates a change of focus. The formation consists of two clauses: the first being A and the second being B, with 'そういえば' in between as a connector.

sentences.jp: 映画。そういえば、彼は映画館で働いているんだ。
sentences.en: Movies. Speaking of which, he works at a movie theater.

sentences.jp: 山田さん。そういえば、彼女は今週末旅行しています。
sentences.en: Ms. Yamada. By the way, she is traveling this weekend.

sentences.jp: そのレストラン。そういえば、次の週末そこでパーティーがあるんだ。
sentences.en: That restaurant. Now that you mention it, there's a party there next weekend.

sentences.jp: 寒い。そういえば、明日雪が降るらしいよ。
sentences.en: It's cold. Speaking of which, it seems like it will snow tomorrow.

title: A。そこで B。(~sokode)
formation: A + そこで + B
shortExplanation: Expresses 'therefore' or 'because of that'.
longExplanation: The A。そこで B。grammar point is used to describe the reason for an action or situation in the A part and the result or decision made in the B part. It can be translated as 'therefore' or 'because of that' in English.

sentences.jp: 明日は試験があります。そこで今晩勉強しようと思います。
sentences.en: There is an exam tomorrow. Therefore, I plan to study tonight.

sentences.jp: このレストランは混んでいます。そこで別の場所でランチを食べましょう。
sentences.en: This restaurant is crowded. Therefore, let's eat lunch at a different place.

sentences.jp: 彼女はアレルギーがある。そこで彼女のために別の料理を作ります。
sentences.en: She has an allergy. Therefore, I'll cook a different dish for her.

sentences.jp: 傘を持っていない。そこで雨が止むまで待ちます。
sentences.en: I don't have an umbrella. Therefore, I'll wait until the rain stops.

title: A。それがB。(~sorega)
formation: A (Statement)。それが B (Restatement or explanation of A)。
shortExplanation: Expressing 'that's exactly what A is; in other words, B'.
longExplanation: The grammar point A。それがB。can be used to restate or clarify that B is precisely what A means. It emphasizes the essence or definition of A by stating B. It can be translated as 'that’s what A is,' 'that is precisely A/B,' or 'in other words, B.'

sentences.jp: 彼は勉強ができる。それが天才というものだ。
sentences.en: He can study well. That's what you call a genius.

sentences.jp: 美味しい料理を作る。それが私の趣味なんです。
sentences.en: I make delicious food. That's my hobby.

sentences.jp: 彼女は私を助けてくれる。それが友達というものだ。
sentences.en: She helps me. That's what a friend is.

sentences.jp: 一人で長い旅をする。それが自由というものだ。
sentences.en: Traveling alone for a long time. That's what freedom is.

title: A。それで B。 (~sore de)
formation: Sentence A +。それで+ Sentence B。
shortExplanation: Express a sequence of events or cause-and-effect; 'and then', 'so', 'because of that'.
longExplanation: The A。それで B。grammar point is used to connect two sentences or events, expressing a sequence of events or explaining the reason for the second event based on the first event. Essentially, it means 'and then', 'so', or 'because of that' in English.

sentences.jp: 昨日は雨が降っていた。それで公園に行かなかった。
sentences.en: It was raining yesterday. So, I didn't go to the park.

sentences.jp: 試験に落ちた。それで再試験を受けなければならない。
sentences.en: I failed the exam. So, I have to take a re-exam.

sentences.jp: 部屋が寒かった。それで暖房をつけた。
sentences.en: The room was cold. So, I turned on the heater.

sentences.jp: 今日は休みだ。それで映画を見に行く。
sentences.en: Today is a holiday. So, I'm going to watch a movie.

title: A。それでも B。(~sore demo)
formation: A (situation) + 。それでも + B (action or thought)
shortExplanation: Expresses 'even so', 'nevertheless', 'still', or 'but'; used to contrast two statements.
longExplanation: The A。それでも B。grammar point is used to show a contrast between two statements. The first statement (A) presents a situation, and the second statement (B) presents an action or thought that might be unexpected or contrary to the initial situation. It can be translated as 'even so', 'nevertheless', 'still', or 'but' in English.

sentences.jp: 今日は日曜日です。それでも、仕事に行かなければなりません。
sentences.en: Today is Sunday. Nevertheless, I have to go to work.

sentences.jp: 彼は若いです。それでも、経験が豊富です。
sentences.en: He is young. Even so, he has a lot of experience.

sentences.jp: 雨が降っています。それでも、散歩に行きたいです。
sentences.en: It's raining. Still, I want to go for a walk.

sentences.jp: 試験の結果が悪かったです。それでも、諦めません。
sentences.en: The exam results were bad. But I won't give up.

title: A。それなのに B。(~sorenanoni)
formation: A。それなのに B。
shortExplanation: Express contrast or a contradiction between two situations or facts; 'even though', 'despite', 'but still'.
longExplanation: The A。それなのにB。grammar point is used to express contrast, contradiction or unexpectedness between two situations or facts. It can be translated as 'even though', 'despite', or 'but still' in English. The formation is simple, with A and B representing the two contrasting or contradicting statements.

sentences.jp: 彼は頭がいい。それなのに、試験に落ちた。
sentences.en: He is smart. Despite that, he failed the exam.

sentences.jp: 私は早く起きた。それなのに、バスに遅れた。
sentences.en: I woke up early. Even so, I was late for the bus.

sentences.jp: 昨日は晴れていた。それなのに、彼は傘を持っていた。
sentences.en: It was sunny yesterday. Despite that, he had an umbrella.

sentences.jp: 彼女は運動が苦手だ。それなのに、マラソンを走ることにした。
sentences.en: She is not good at sports. Nevertheless, she decided to run a marathon.

id: N2-A。それなら B。(A. Sore nara B.)
title: A。それなら B。(A. Sore nara B.)
formation: Situation A (sentence) + 。それなら + Situation B (sentence)
shortExplanation: Express 'if that's the case, then'; used to show a logical consequence or decision based on a given situation.
longExplanation: The A。それなら B。grammar point is used to express a logical consequence or decision based on the given situation in the first part of the sentence (A). The phrase それなら can be translated as 'if that's the case', 'in that case', or 'then' in English.

sentences.jp: 試験が近い。それなら、もっと勉強しなければならない。
sentences.en: The exam is coming soon. If that's the case, I must study more.

sentences.jp: 明日は雨が降るだろう。それなら、傘を持って行こう。
sentences.en: It will probably rain tomorrow. In that case, let's bring an umbrella.

sentences.jp: 彼らはもう帰った。それなら、私も帰ろう。
sentences.en: They have already left. If that's the case, I'll go home too.

sentences.jp: あのレストランは予約がいっぱいだ。それなら、別の場所を探そう。
sentences.en: Reservations at that restaurant are full. In that case, let's find another place.

title: A。だが B。(~daga)
formation: Sentence A + だが + Sentence B
shortExplanation: Expresses contrasting ideas or statements; 'but', 'however'.
longExplanation: The A。だが B。grammar point is used to express contrasting ideas or statements. It connects two sentences or phrases with a contrasting meaning. It can be translated as 'but' or 'however' in English.

sentences.jp: このレストランは値段が高い。だが、味はとてもいい。
sentences.en: This restaurant is expensive. But, the taste is very good.

sentences.jp: 彼は疲れている。だが、仕事を終わらせるまで休まない。
sentences.en: He is tired. However, he will not rest until he finishes work.

sentences.jp: 昨日は寒かった。だが、今日は暖かい。
sentences.en: Yesterday was cold. But, today is warm.

sentences.jp: 彼女は外見は地味だ。だが、性格が素晴らしい。
sentences.en: Her appearance is plain. However, she has a wonderful personality.

title: A。ただB。(~tada)
formation: Sentence A + ただ + Sentence B
shortExplanation: Express contrast between two situations; 'although', 'but', 'however'.
longExplanation: The ただ grammar point is used to express contrast between two situations or facts. It can often be translated as 'although', 'but', or 'however' in English. It is used to show a contradiction or a different aspect of a situation.

sentences.jp: 彼は頭がいい。ただ、努力はあまりしない。
sentences.en: He is smart; however, he doesn't make much effort.

sentences.jp: 外は雨が降っている。ただ、家にいると暇だ。
sentences.en: It's raining outside, but I'm bored at home.

sentences.jp: そのレストランは値段が高い。ただ、サービスが良い。
sentences.en: That restaurant is expensive; however, the service is good.

sentences.jp: テストの結果は良くなかった。ただ、やり直すチャンスがある。
sentences.en: The test results were not good, but there's a chance to retake it.

title: A。ただしB。 (A. Tadashi B)
formation: Statement A + ただし + Condition B
shortExplanation: Adding a condition or exception to a statement; 'however', 'provided that', 'but only if'.
longExplanation: The grammar point A。ただしB is used to add a condition or exception to a previous statement. It can be translated to 'however', 'provided that', or 'but only if' in English. This structure consists of two parts: A (statement) and B (condition/exception). The word ただし is used to connect these two parts.

sentences.jp: この部屋を使ってもいいです。ただし、使った後で掃除してください。
sentences.en: You can use this room. However, please clean up after you use it.

sentences.jp: 明日から出張で不在になります。ただし、緊急の場合は携帯に連絡してください。
sentences.en: I'll be away on a business trip starting tomorrow. However, in case of emergency, please contact me on my cell phone.

sentences.jp: 食事代は全額会社が負担します。ただし、アルコールのみ自己負担となります。
sentences.en: The company will cover the entire meal cost. However, alcohol expenses will be borne by the individual.

sentences.jp: 図書館で本を借りることができます。ただし、期限内に返さなければなりません。
sentences.en: You can borrow books from the library. However, you must return them within the deadline.

title: A。だって B。(Datte~)
formation: Verb-casual + だって、い-Adjective + だって、な-Adjective + だって、Noun + だって
shortExplanation: Used to give a reason, express cause or bring up a topic; 'because', 'since', 'it's just that'.
longExplanation: The A。だって B。grammar point is used to give a reason, express cause, or introduce a topic in a casual manner. It can be translated as 'because', 'since', or 'it's just that'. A is the reason and B is the statement that follows due to the reason. It can be used with verbs, い-adjectives, な-adjectives, and nouns.

sentences.jp: 暇だって、映画を見に行こうよ。
sentences.en: Since we are free, let's go watch a movie.

sentences.jp: 寒いだって、コートを着てください。
sentences.en: Because it's cold, please wear a coat.

sentences.jp: 疲れているだって、休んだほうがいい。
sentences.en: Since you're tired, it's better to rest.

sentences.jp: 彼女が料理上手だって、みんな彼女の作った料理を楽しみにしています。
sentences.en: Because she is good at cooking, everyone is looking forward to her dishes.

id: N2-A。ちなみに B。(A. Chinamini B.)
title: A。ちなみに B。(A. Chinamini B.)
formation: A。ちなみに B。
shortExplanation: Used to give additional information about the topic; 'by the way', 'incidentally'.
longExplanation: The ちなみに grammar point is used to provide additional information or a side note about a topic that has been previously mentioned. It can be translated as 'by the way' or 'incidentally' in English. This grammar point is typically used to give further context, background information, or a slight change in topic.

sentences.jp: 今日は授業がありません。ちなみに、明日も休みです。
sentences.en: There's no class today. By the way, tomorrow is also a day off.

sentences.jp: この店のラーメンは美味しいです。ちなみに、餃子もおすすめです。
sentences.en: The ramen at this shop is delicious. By the way, their gyoza is also recommended.

sentences.jp: 彼は今日は遅刻しました。ちなみに、昨日は欠席しました。
sentences.en: He was late today. By the way, he was absent yesterday.

sentences.jp: 映画は面白かったです。ちなみに、原作の方がもっといいですよ。
sentences.en: The movie was interesting. By the way, the original work is even better.

id: N2-A。ということは B。 (A. To iu koto wa B.)
title: A。ということは B。 (A. To iu koto wa B.)
formation: A + ということは + B
shortExplanation: Expresses the meaning 'if A, that means B' or 'A implies B'.
longExplanation: The A。ということは B。grammar point is used to express the logical conclusion that if A is true, then B must also be true. It can be translated as 'if A, that means B' or 'A implies B' in English. This pattern is used when the speaker wants to infer or deduce B based on the information provided by A.

sentences.jp: 彼女が泣いている。ということは何か悲しいことがあったに違いない。
sentences.en: She is crying. That means something sad must have happened.

sentences.jp: このケーキが残っている。ということは、みんなお腹がいっぱいだったのかな。
sentences.en: This cake is left over. That means everyone must have been full, I guess.

sentences.jp: 道が渋滞している。ということは、事故があったのかもしれない。
sentences.en: The road is congested. That means there might have been an accident.

sentences.jp: 彼が試験に受かった。ということは、勉強がたくさん頑張ったんだね。
sentences.en: He passed the exam. That means he must have studied really hard.

id: N2-A。というのは B。(Toiu no wa~)
title: A。というのは B。(Toiu no wa~)
formation: [Statement A]。というのは、[Statement B]。
shortExplanation: Used to give an explanation or provide additional information for A; 'that is to say', 'in other words'.
longExplanation: The grammar point A。というのは B。 is used to provide an explanation, definition, or further information about statement A. It can be translated as 'that is to say', 'in other words', or 'namely' in English.

sentences.jp: 彼は非常に疲れています。というのは、昨夜、車で遠くまで行ってきたためです。
sentences.en: He is very tired. That is to say, because he went far by car last night.

sentences.jp: このレストランはおすすめです。というのは、料理が美味しいし、雰囲気もいいからです。
sentences.en: I recommend this restaurant. That is to say, because the food is delicious and the atmosphere is nice.

sentences.jp: 試験に落ちた。というのは、十分に勉強しなかったからです。
sentences.en: I failed the exam. In other words, because I didn't study enough.

sentences.jp: 彼女は独特な趣味がある。というのは、昆虫収集だ。
sentences.en: She has a unique hobby. Namely, insect collecting.

title: A。なおB。(A. Nao B.)
formation: A (sentence) + なお + B (sentence)
shortExplanation: Provides additional information; 'moreover', 'besides', 'in addition'.
longExplanation: The なお grammar point is used to provide additional information or emphasize an existing fact. It can be translated as 'moreover', 'besides', or 'in addition' in English. なお is placed between two sentences to connect them and provide supplementary information.

sentences.jp: 明日は休みです。なお、月曜日も休みです。
sentences.en: Tomorrow is a holiday. Moreover, Monday is also a holiday.

sentences.jp: 彼は頭が良いです。なお、スポーツも得意です。
sentences.en: He is smart. Besides, he is also good at sports.

sentences.jp: この本は面白いです。なお、安いです。
sentences.en: This book is interesting. In addition, it is cheap.

sentences.jp: 私は日本語を勉強しています。なお、韓国語も勉強しています。
sentences.en: I am studying Japanese. Moreover, I am also studying Korean.

title: A。もっとも B。(Motto mo ~)
formation: Clause A + もっとも + Clause B
shortExplanation: Express contrasting opinions or facts; 'although', 'but', 'however'.
longExplanation: The A。もっとも B。 grammar point is used to express contrasting opinions or facts. It can be translated as 'although', 'but', or 'however' in English. This structure connects two clauses and highlights the contrast between them.

sentences.jp: 彼は頭がいい。もっとも、勉強も大切だ。
sentences.en: He is smart. However, studying is also important.

sentences.jp: このレストランは美味しい。もっとも、値段が高い。
sentences.en: This restaurant is delicious. But, the prices are high.

sentences.jp: 彼女は綺麗だ。もっとも、性格が悪い。
sentences.en: She is beautiful. Although, her personality is bad.

sentences.jp: 日本は面白い国だ。もっとも、住むのが大変そうだ。
sentences.en: Japan is an interesting country. But, it seems difficult to live in.

id: N2-A。要するに B。(A. Yousuru ni B.)
title: A。要するに B。(A. Yousuru ni B.)
formation: A。要するに B。
shortExplanation: Expressing 'in short', 'in a nutshell', or 'to sum up' when restating or summarizing A as B.
longExplanation: The A。要するに B。grammar point is used to restate or summarize the information provided in A more concisely as B. It can be translated as 'in short', 'in a nutshell', or 'to sum up' in English. The formation consists of two sentences or statements, A and B, with 要するに (yousuru ni) in between to indicate the summary or restatement of A as B.

sentences.jp: このプロジェクトは成功しました。要するに、私たちのチームは素晴らしい仕事をしました。
sentences.en: This project was successful. In short, our team did a great job.

sentences.jp: 彼は料理が上手です。要するに、彼は優れたシェフです。
sentences.en: He is good at cooking. In a nutshell, he is an excellent chef.

sentences.jp: 昨日はとても忙しかった。要するに、一日中働いていました。
sentences.en: Yesterday was very busy. To sum up, I was working all day.

sentences.jp: 彼女は新しい仕事が好きです。要するに、彼女は満足しています。
sentences.en: She likes her new job. In short, she is satisfied.

title: Noun につき (〜ni tsuki)
formation: Noun + につき
shortExplanation: Indicates the reason, cause, or condition for something; 'due to', 'regarding', 'per'.
longExplanation: The につき grammar point is used to express the reason, cause, or condition for an event or situation. In English, it can be translated as 'due to', 'regarding', 'per', or 'for each'. It can be used with nouns.

sentences.jp: 試験につき、今日は静かにしてください。
sentences.en: Due to the exam, please be quiet today.

sentences.jp: このエリアは工事中につき、通行止めです。
sentences.en: The area is closed for passage due to construction.

sentences.jp: 博物館は整備中につき、入場料が無料です。
sentences.en: Due to maintenance, admission to the museum is free.

sentences.jp: 商品お一つにつき、1000円の割引があります。
sentences.en: There is a 1,000 yen discount per item.

title: Noun にて (Noun nite)
formation: Noun + にて
shortExplanation: Expresses location or means; 'at', 'in', 'by', 'with'.
longExplanation: The にて grammar point is used to indicate the location, means, or method of an action. It can be translated as 'at', 'in', 'by', or 'with' in English. It functions similarly to the particle で, but にて is more formal and often used in written language, formal speaking, and announcements.

sentences.jp: 駅前広場にてイベントが開催されます。
sentences.en: An event will be held at the station square.

sentences.jp: ホテルにて会議が行われます。
sentences.en: The meeting will take place at the hotel.

sentences.jp: 郵便局にて荷物を受け取りました。
sentences.en: I received the package at the post office.

sentences.jp: インターネットにて調べたら、答えが見つかりました。
sentences.en: I searched online and found the answer.

id: N2-Noun の ことだから (Noun no koto dakara)
title: Noun の ことだから (Noun no koto dakara)
formation: Noun + のことだから
shortExplanation: Used to provide a reason or explanation based on one's knowledge about the noun; 'since', 'because', 'knowing'.
longExplanation: The の ことだから (no koto dakara) grammar point is used when giving a reason or explanation based on one’s knowledge or understanding of a particular person or thing. It often implies 'given that this is the kind of person/thing it is'.

sentences.jp: 料理が得意な彼のことだから、美味しい料理を作ってくれるでしょう。
sentences.en: Since he’s good at cooking, he’ll probably make delicious food.

sentences.jp: いつも遅刻する彼女のことだから、今日も遅れるかもしれない。
sentences.en: Because she’s always late, she might be late again today.

sentences.jp: 安い店のことだから、たくさん売れるはずだ。
sentences.en: Since it’s a cheap store, it should sell a lot.

sentences.jp: ドラマ好きな彼のことだから、最新のドラマについて話すといいでしょう。
